PUBLIC_CHANNEL is the public chat channel that all avatars see as chat text. DEBUG_CHANNEL is the script debug channel, and is also visible to nearby avatars. All other channels are are not sent to avatars, but may be used to communicate with scripts.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.SignRSA.documentation: Returns the base64-encoded RSA signature of Message using PEM-formatted PrivateKey and digest Algorithm (sha1, sha224, sha256, sha384, sha512).

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.Sin.documentation: Returns the sine of Theta (Theta in radians).

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.SitOnLink.documentation: If agent identified by AvatarID is participating in the experience, sit them on the specified link's sit target.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.SitTarget.documentation: Set the sit location for this object. If offset == ZERO_VECTOR, clears the sit target.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.Sleep.documentation: Put script to sleep for Time seconds.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.Sound.documentation:
Deprecated: Use llPlaySound instead.
Plays Sound at Volume and specifies whether the sound should loop and/or be enqueued.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.SoundPreload.documentation:
Deprecated: Use llPreloadSound instead.
Preloads a sound on viewers within range.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.Sqrt.documentation:
Returns the square root of Value.
Triggers a math runtime error for imaginary results (if Value < 0.0).

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.StartAnimation.documentation:
This function plays the specified animation from playing on the avatar who received the script's most recent permissions request.
Animation may be an animation in task inventory or a built-in animation.
Requires the PERMISSION_TRIGGER_ANIMATION runtime permission (automatically granted to attached or sat on objects).

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.StartObjectAnim.documentation:
This function plays the specified animation on the rigged mesh object associated with the current script.
Animation may be an animation in task inventory or a built-in animation.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.StopAnimation.documentation:
This function stops the specified animation on the avatar who received the script's most recent permissions request.
Animation may be an animation in task inventory, a built-in animation, or the uuid of an animation.
Requires the PERMISSION_TRIGGER_ANIMATION runtime permission (automatically granted to attached or sat on objects).

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.StopHover.documentation: Stop hovering to a height (due to llSetHoverHeight()).

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.StopLookAt.documentation: Stop causing object to point at a target (due to llLookAt() or llRotLookAt()).

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.StopMoveToTarge.documentation: Stops critically damped motion (due to llMoveToTarget()).

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.StopObjectAnima.documentation:
This function stops the specified animation on the rigged mesh object associated with the current script.
Animation may be an animation in task inventory, a built-in animation, or the uuid of an animation.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.StopSound.documentation: Stops playback of the currently attached sound.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.StringLength.documentation: Returns an integer that is the number of characters in Text (not counting the null).

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.StringToBase64.documentation: Returns the string Base64 representation of the input string.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.StringTrim.documentation:
Outputs a string, eliminating white-space from the start and/or end of the input string Text.
Valid options for TrimType:
STRING_TRIM_HEAD: trim all leading spaces in Text
STRING_TRIM_TAIL: trim all trailing spaces in Text
STRING_TRIM: trim all leading and trailing spaces in Text.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.SubStringIndex.documentation: (Index semantics) Returns the first index where Sequence appears in Text. Returns -1 if not found.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.TakeControls.documentation:
Take controls from the agent the script has permissions for.
If (Accept == (Controls & input)), send input to the script. PassOn determines whether Controls also perform their normal functions.
Requires the PERMISSION_TAKE_CONTROLS runtime permission (automatically granted to attached or sat on objects).

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.Tan.documentation: Returns the tangent of Theta (Theta in radians).

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.Target.documentation:
This function is to have the script know when it has reached a position.
It registers a Position with a Range that triggers at_target and not_at_target events continuously until unregistered.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.TargetOmega.documentation:
Attempt to spin at SpinRate with strength Gain on Axis.
A spin rate of 0.0 cancels the spin. This function always works in object-local coordinates.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.TargetRemove.documentation: Removes positional target Handle registered with llTarget.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.TargetedEmail.documentation: Sends an email with Subject and Message to the owner or creator of an object.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.TeleportAgent.documentation:
Requests a teleport of avatar to a landmark stored in the object's inventory. If no landmark is provided (an empty string), the avatar is teleported to the location position in the current region. In either case, the avatar is turned to face the position given by look_at in local coordinates.
Requires the PERMISSION_TELEPORT runtime permission.
This function can only teleport the owner of the object.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.TeleportAgentGl.documentation:
Teleports an agent to the RegionPosition local coordinates within a region which is specified by the GlobalPosition global coordinates. The agent lands facing the position defined by LookAtPoint local coordinates.
Requires the PERMISSION_TELEPORT runtime permission.
This function can only teleport the owner of the object.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.TeleportAgentHo.documentation: Teleport agent over the owner's land to agent's home location.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.TextBox.documentation: Opens a dialog for the specified avatar with message Text, which contains a text box for input. Any text that is entered is said on the specified Channel (as if by the avatar) when the "OK" button is clicked.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.ToLower.documentation: Returns a string that is Text with all lower-case characters.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.ToUpper.documentation: Returns a string that is Text with all upper-case characters.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.TransferLindenD.documentation:
Transfer Amount of linden dollars (L$) from script owner to AvatarID. Returns a key to a corresponding transaction_result event for the success of the transfer.
Attempts to send the amount of money to the specified avatar, and trigger a transaction_result event identified by the returned key. Requires the PERMISSION_DEBIT runtime permission.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.TransferOwnersh.documentation: Transfers ownership of an object, or a copy of the object to a new agent.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.TriggerSound.documentation:
Plays Sound at Volume (0.0 - 1.0), centered at but not attached to object.
There is no limit to the number of triggered sounds which can be generated by an object, and calling llTriggerSound does not affect the attached sounds created by llPlaySound and llLoopSound. This is very useful for things like collision noises, explosions, etc. There is no way to stop or alter the volume of a sound triggered by this function.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.TriggerSoundLim.documentation:
Plays Sound at Volume (0.0 - 1.0), centered at but not attached to object, limited to axis-aligned bounding box defined by vectors top-north-east (TNE) and bottom-south-west (BSW).
There is no limit to the number of triggered sounds which can be generated by an object, and calling llTriggerSound does not affect the attached sounds created by llPlaySound and llLoopSound. This is very useful for things like collision noises, explosions, etc. There is no way to stop or alter the volume of a sound triggered by this function.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.UnSit.documentation: If agent identified by AvatarID is sitting on the object the script is attached to or is over land owned by the object's owner, the agent is forced to stand up.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.UnescapeURL.documentation:
Returns the string that is the URL unescaped, replacing "%20" with spaces, etc., version of URL.
This function can output raw UTF-8 strings.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.UpdateCharacter.documentation: Updates settings for a pathfinding character.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.UpdateKeyValue.documentation: Starts an asychronous transaction to update the value associated with the key given. The dataserver callback will be executed with the key returned from this call and a string describing the result. The result is a two element commma-delimited list. The first item is an integer specifying if the transaction succeeded (1) or not (0). In the failure case, the second item will be an integer corresponding to one of the XP_ERROR_... constants. In the success case the second item will be the value associated with the key. If Checked is 1 the existing value in the data store must match the OriginalValue passed or XP_ERROR_RETRY_UPDATE will be returned. If Checked is 0 the key will be created if necessary.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.VecDist.documentation: Returns the distance between Location1 and Location2.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.VecMag.documentation: Returns the magnitude of the vector.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.VerifyRSA.documentation: Returns TRUE if PublicKey, Message, and Algorithm produce the same base64-formatted Signature.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.VolumeDetect.documentation:
If DetectEnabled = TRUE, object becomes phantom but triggers collision_start and collision_end events when other objects start and stop interpenetrating.
If another object (including avatars) interpenetrates it, it will get a collision_start event.
When an object stops interpenetrating, a collision_end event is generated. While the other is inter-penetrating, collision events are NOT generated.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.WanderWithin.documentation:
Wander within a specified volume.
Sets a character to wander about a central spot within a specified area.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.Water.documentation: Returns the water height below the object position + Offset.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.Whisper.documentation:
Whispers Text on Channel.
This chat method has a range of 10m radius.
PUBLIC_CHANNEL is the public chat channel that all avatars see as chat text. DEBUG_CHANNEL is the script debug channel, and is also visible to nearby avatars. All other channels are are not sent to avatars, but may be used to communicate with scripts.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.Wind.documentation: Returns the wind velocity at the object position + Offset.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.WorldPosToHUD.documentation: Returns the local position that would put the origin of a HUD object directly over world_pos as viewed by the current camera. Requires the PERMISSION_TRACK_CAMERA runtime permission (else will return zero vector).

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.XorBase64.documentation: Performs an exclusive OR on two Base64 strings and returns a Base64 string. Text2 repeats if it is shorter than Text1.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.XorBase64String.documentation:
Deprecated: Please use llXorBase64 instead.
Incorrectly performs an exclusive OR on two Base64 strings and returns a Base64 string. Text2 repeats if it is shorter than Text1.
Retained for backwards compatibility.

Deprecated: Please use llXorBase64 instead.
Correctly (unless nulls are present) performs an exclusive OR on two Base64 strings and returns a Base64 string.
Text2 repeats if it is shorter than Text1.

@sl-slua/global/llcompat.sRGB2Linear.documentation: Converts a color from the sRGB to the linear colorspace.

@sl-slua/global/ACTIVE.documentation:
Value: 0x2
Objects running a script or physically moving (using server resources). In llDetectedType(), it identifies physical objects and agents. In llSensor() or llSensorRepeat(), it searches for moving physical objects or scripted objects.

@sl-slua/global/AGENT.documentation:
Value: 0x1
Agents (avatars). In llDetectedType, it indicates an avatar. In llSensor or llSensorRepeat, it searches for avatars by legacy name (functionally identical to AGENT_BY_LEGACY_NAME, though using AGENT_BY_LEGACY_NAME is recommended instead).

@sl-slua/global/AGENT_ALWAYS_RUN.documentation:
Value: 0x1000
Used with llGetAgentInfo to determine if the queried avatar has 'Always Run' enabled or is using tap-tap-hold.

@sl-slua/global/AGENT_ATTACHMENTS.documentation:
Value: 0x2
Used with llGetAgentInfo to determine if the queried avatar has attachments.

@sl-slua/global/AGENT_AUTOMATED.documentation:
Value: 0x4000
Used with llGetAgentInfo to identify if the avatar is registered with Linden Lab as an automated/scripted agent (bot).

@sl-slua/global/AGENT_AUTOPILOT.documentation:
Value: 0x2000
Used with llGetAgentInfo to determine if the avatar is in autopilot mode, which is enabled when the user selects 'Go Here' on the ground or uses double-click autopilot.

@sl-slua/global/AGENT_AWAY.documentation:
Value: 0x40
Used with llGetAgentInfo to determine if the avatar is in 'away' mode, which indicates they toggled away or have been inactive for too long.

@sl-slua/global/AGENT_BUSY.documentation:
Value: 0x800
Used with llGetAgentInfo to determine if the avatar is in 'busy' mode.

@sl-slua/global/AGENT_BY_LEGACY_NAME.documentation:
Value: 0x1
Used to find agents by legacy name. In llDetectedType, it indicates an avatar. In llSensor or llSensorRepeat, it searches for avatars by legacy name.

@sl-slua/global/AGENT_BY_USERNAME.documentation:
Value: 0x10
Used to find agents by username (see Avatar Names).

@sl-slua/global/AGENT_CROUCHING.documentation:
Value: 0x400
Used with llGetAgentInfo to determine if the avatar is crouching.

@sl-slua/global/AGENT_FLOATING_VIA_SCRIP.documentation:
Value: 0x8000
Used with llGetAgentInfo to determine if the avatar is floating or hovering because of a scripted attachment using either llSetHoverHeight or llGroundRepel.

@sl-slua/global/AGENT_FLYING.documentation:
Value: 0x1
Used with llGetAgentInfo to determine if the queried avatar is flying or hovering.

@sl-slua/global/AGENT_IN_AIR.documentation:
Value: 0x100
Used with llGetAgentInfo to determine if the avatar is in the air (jumping, flying, or falling).

@sl-slua/global/AGENT_LIST_PARCEL.documentation:
Value: 1
Agents on the same parcel where the script is running.

@sl-slua/global/AGENT_LIST_PARCEL_OWNER.documentation:
Value: 2
Agents on any parcel in the region where the parcel owner is the same as the owner of the parcel under the scripted object.

@sl-slua/global/AGENT_LIST_REGION.documentation:
Value: 4
Returns any or all agents in the region.

@sl-slua/global/AGENT_MOUSELOOK.documentation:
Value: 0x8
Used with llGetAgentInfo to determine if the avatar is in mouselook.

@sl-slua/global/AGENT_ON_OBJECT.documentation:
Value: 0x20
Used with llGetAgentInfo to determine if the avatar is sitting on an object and linked to it.

@sl-slua/global/AGENT_SCRIPTED.documentation:
Value: 0x4
Used with llGetAgentInfo to determine if the avatar is carrying scripted objects or has scripted attachments.

@sl-slua/global/AGENT_SITTING.documentation:
Value: 0x10
Used with llGetAgentInfo to determine if the avatar is sitting.

@sl-slua/global/AGENT_TYPING.documentation:
Value: 0x200
Used with llGetAgentInfo to determine if the avatar is typing.

@sl-slua/global/AGENT_WALKING.documentation:
Value: 0x80
Used with llGetAgentInfo to determine if the queried avatar is walking, running, or crouch walking.

@sl-slua/global/ALL_SIDES.documentation:
Value: -1
Selects all sides of an object in an applicable function.

@sl-slua/global/ANIM_ON.documentation:
Value: 0x1
Enables texture animation. This must be set to start the animation and cleared to stop it.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_ANY_HUD.documentation:
Value: -1
A special constant representing all HUD attachment points when filtering for any HUD attachment.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_AVATAR_CENTER.documentation:
Value: 40
Attachment point for the avatar's geometric center or root.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_BACK.documentation:
Value: 9
Attachment point for the avatar's back.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_BELLY.documentation:
Value: 28
Attachment point for the avatar's belly, stomach, or tummy.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_CHEST.documentation:
Value: 1
Attachment point for the avatar's chest or sternum.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_CHIN.documentation:
Value: 12
Attachment point for the avatar's chin.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_FACE_JAW.documentation:
Value: 47
Attachment point for the avatar's jaw.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_FACE_LEAR.documentation:
Value: 48
Attachment point for the avatar's left ear (extended).

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_FACE_LEYE.documentation:
Value: 50
Attachment point for the avatar's left eye (extended).

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_FACE_REAR.documentation:
Value: 49
Attachment point for the avatar's right ear (extended).

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_FACE_REYE.documentation:
Value: 51
Attachment point for the avatar's right eye (extended).

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_FACE_TONGUE.documentation:
Value: 52
Attachment point for the avatar's tongue.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_GROIN.documentation:
Value: 53
Attachment point for the avatar's groin.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_HEAD.documentation:
Value: 2
Attachment point for the avatar's head.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_HIND_LFOOT.documentation:
Value: 54
Attachment point for the avatar's left hind foot.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_HIND_RFOOT.documentation:
Value: 55
Attachment point for the avatar's right hind foot.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_HUD_BOTTOM_LEFT.documentation:
Value: 36
Attachment point for HUD Bottom Left.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_HUD_BOTTOM_RIGHT.documentation:
Value: 38
Attachment point for HUD Bottom Right.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_HUD_TOP_CENTER.documentation:
Value: 33
Attachment point for HUD Top Center.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_HUD_TOP_LEFT.documentation:
Value: 34
Attachment point for HUD Top Left.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_HUD_TOP_RIGHT.documentation:
Value: 32
Attachment point for HUD Top Right.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_LEAR.documentation:
Value: 13
Attachment point for the avatar's left ear.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_LEFT_PEC.documentation:
Value: 29
Attachment point for the avatar's left pectoral.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_LEYE.documentation:
Value: 15
Attachment point for the avatar's left eye.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_LFOOT.documentation:
Value: 7
Attachment point for the avatar's left foot.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_LHAND.documentation:
Value: 5
Attachment point for the avatar's left hand.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_LHAND_RING1.documentation:
Value: 41
Attachment point for the avatar's left ring finger.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_LHIP.documentation:
Value: 25
Attachment point for the avatar's left hip.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_LLARM.documentation:
Value: 21
Attachment point for the avatar's left lower arm.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_LLLEG.documentation:
Value: 27
Attachment point for the avatar's left lower leg.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_LPEC.documentation:
Value: 30
Attachment point for the avatar's right pectoral (deprecated, use ATTACH_RIGHT_PEC).

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_LSHOULDER.documentation:
Value: 3
Attachment point for the avatar's left shoulder.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_LUARM.documentation:
Value: 20
Attachment point for the avatar's left upper arm.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_LULEG.documentation:
Value: 26
Attachment point for the avatar's left upper leg.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_LWING.documentation:
Value: 45
Attachment point for the avatar's left wing.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_MOUTH.documentation:
Value: 11
Attachment point for the avatar's mouth.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_NECK.documentation:
Value: 39
Attachment point for the avatar's neck.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_NOSE.documentation:
Value: 17
Attachment point for the avatar's nose.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_PELVIS.documentation:
Value: 10
Attachment point for the avatar's pelvis.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_REAR.documentation:
Value: 14
Attachment point for the avatar's right ear.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_REYE.documentation:
Value: 16
Attachment point for the avatar's right eye.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_RFOOT.documentation:
Value: 8
Attachment point for the avatar's right foot.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_RHAND.documentation:
Value: 6
Attachment point for the avatar's right hand (the default attachment point).

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_RHAND_RING1.documentation:
Value: 42
Attachment point for the avatar's right ring finger.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_RHIP.documentation:
Value: 22
Attachment point for the avatar's right hip.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_RIGHT_PEC.documentation:
Value: 30
Attachment point for the avatar's right pectoral.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_RLARM.documentation:
Value: 19
Attachment point for the avatar's right lower arm.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_RLLEG.documentation:
Value: 24
Attachment point for the avatar's right lower leg.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_RPEC.documentation:
Value: 29
Attachment point for the avatar's left pectoral (deprecated, use ATTACH_LEFT_PEC).

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_RSHOULDER.documentation:
Value: 4
Attachment point for the avatar's right shoulder.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_RUARM.documentation:
Value: 18
Attachment point for the avatar's right upper arm.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_RULEG.documentation:
Value: 23
Attachment point for the avatar's right upper leg.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_RWING.documentation:
Value: 46
Attachment point for the avatar's right wing.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_TAIL_BASE.documentation:
Value: 43
Attachment point for the avatar's tail base.

@sl-slua/global/ATTACH_TAIL_TIP.documentation:
Value: 44
Attachment point for the avatar's tail tip.

@sl-slua/global/BEACON_MAP.documentation:
Value: 1
Causes llMapBeacon to optionally display and focus the world map on the avatar's viewer.

@sl-slua/global/CAMERA_ACTIVE.documentation:
Value: 12
Option flag used with llSetCameraParams to turn scripted control of the camera on or off.

@sl-slua/global/CAMERA_BEHINDNESS_ANGLE.documentation:
Value: 8
Sets the angle in degrees within which the camera is not constrained by changes in target rotation.

@sl-slua/global/CAMERA_BEHINDNESS_LAG.documentation:
Value: 9
Sets how strongly the camera is forced to stay behind the target if outside of the behindness angle.

@sl-slua/global/CAMERA_DISTANCE.documentation:
Value: 7
Sets how far away the camera wants to be from its target.

@sl-slua/global/CAMERA_FOCUS.documentation:
Value: 17
Sets camera focus (target position) in region coordinates.

@sl-slua/global/CAMERA_FOCUS_LAG.documentation:
Value: 6
Sets how much the camera lags as it aims toward the target.

@sl-slua/global/CAMERA_FOCUS_LOCKED.documentation:
Value: 22
Option flag used with llSetCameraParams to lock the camera focus so it does not move.

@sl-slua/global/CAMERA_FOCUS_OFFSET.documentation:
Value: 1
Adjusts the camera focus position relative to the target.

@sl-slua/global/CAMERA_FOCUS_THRESHOLD.documentation:
Value: 11
Sets the radius of a sphere around the camera's target position within which its focus is not affected by target motion.

@sl-slua/global/CAMERA_PITCH.documentation:
Value: 0
Adjusts the angular amount that the camera aims straight ahead versus straight down, maintaining the same distance (analogous to 'incidence').

@sl-slua/global/CAMERA_POSITION.documentation:
Value: 13
Sets camera position in region coordinates.

@sl-slua/global/CAMERA_POSITION_LAG.documentation:
Value: 5
Sets how much the camera lags as it moves toward its ideal position.

@sl-slua/global/CAMERA_POSITION_LOCKED.documentation:
Value: 21
Option flag used with llSetCameraParams to lock the camera position so it does not move.

@sl-slua/global/CAMERA_POSITION_THRESHOL.documentation:
Value: 10
Sets the radius of a sphere around the camera's ideal position within which it is not affected by target motion.

@sl-slua/global/CHANGED_ALLOWED_DROP.documentation:
Value: 0x40
The object inventory changed because a user other than the owner (or the owner if the object is no-mod) added an item. This is only possible if enabled via llAllowInventoryDrop.

@sl-slua/global/CHANGED_COLOR.documentation:
Value: 0x2
The object's Blinn-Phong color or alpha parameters have changed.

@sl-slua/global/CHANGED_INVENTORY.documentation:
Value: 0x1
The prim inventory has changed by someone who has modification rights to it.

@sl-slua/global/CHANGED_LINK.documentation:
Value: 0x20
The number of prims making up the object or avatars seated on it has changed. Also occurs when duplicating a linked object or when a prim changes type or shape. Does not trigger on attach/detach, sit/unsit in attachments, or single prim duplication.

@sl-slua/global/CHANGED_MEDIA.documentation:
Value: 0x800
Prim Media on the prim has changed.

@sl-slua/global/CHANGED_OWNER.documentation:
Value: 0x80
The object has changed owners. Triggers in the original object when a user takes it, copies it, or deeds it to a group, and in the new object when it is first rezzed.

@sl-slua/global/CHANGED_REGION.documentation:
Value: 0x100
The object has changed regions by crossing a boundary or teleporting (if attached). Triggers only in the root prim of a linkset.

@sl-slua/global/CHANGED_REGION_START.documentation:
Value: 0x400
The region containing this object has just come online.

@sl-slua/global/CHANGED_RENDER_MATERIAL.documentation:
Value: 0x1000
The render material (prim material ID or material overrides) has changed on one or more faces.

@sl-slua/global/CHANGED_SCALE.documentation:
Value: 0x8
The prim scale of at least one prim in the linked object has changed. Only the root prim receives this event.

@sl-slua/global/CHANGED_SHAPE.documentation:
Value: 0x4
The prim base shape (PRIM_TYPE, such as box, prism, torus, taper, twist, or cut) has changed.

@sl-slua/global/CHANGED_TELEPORT.documentation:
Value: 0x200
The avatar this object is attached to has teleported. Triggers only in the root prim of an attachment. Does not occur for child prims or 'sit teleports'. If scripts are disabled at the destination, the event queues and triggers after moving to a script-enabled parcel.

@sl-slua/global/CHANGED_TEXTURE.documentation:
Value: 0x10
The prim texture parameters (shine/bump settings, repeats, flip, rotation, offset, or texture) have changed.

@sl-slua/global/CHARACTER_ACCOUNT_FOR_SK.documentation:
Value: 14
If set to FALSE, the pathfinding character will not attempt to catch up on lost time when performance is low, potentially providing more reliable (though potentially more stuttery) movement. Default is TRUE.

@sl-slua/global/CHARACTER_AVOIDANCE_MODE.documentation:
Value: 5
Specifies which objects a pathfinding character avoids (other characters, dynamic obstacles like fast-moving objects and avatars, or both). Combined using bit flags; setting to AVOID_NONE disables avoidance for both categories.

@sl-slua/global/CHARACTER_CMD_JUMP.documentation:
Value: 0x01
Makes the character jump. Requires a height parameter between 0.1m and 2.0m as the first element of the llExecCharacterCmd option list.

@sl-slua/global/CHARACTER_CMD_SMOOTH_STO.documentation:
Value: 2
Stops any current pathfinding operation in a smooth fashion.

@sl-slua/global/CHARACTER_DESIRED_SPEED.documentation:
Value: 1
Constant used to indicate that the following argument is the desired speed (or speed of pursuit in meters per second) for a pathfinding character.

@sl-slua/global/CHARACTER_DESIRED_TURN_S.documentation:
Value: 12
Specifies the character's maximum speed while turning about the Z-axis (default is 6 meters/second). Note that this is only loosely enforced, and a character may turn at higher speeds under certain conditions.

@sl-slua/global/CHARACTER_LENGTH.documentation:
Value: 3
Sets the collision capsule length for a pathfinding character. If the value is less than twice the radius plus 0.1m, it automatically adjusts to twice the radius plus 0.1m.

@sl-slua/global/CHARACTER_MAX_ACCEL.documentation:
Value: 8
Specifies the character's maximum acceleration rate (default is 20 m/s²).

@sl-slua/global/CHARACTER_MAX_DECEL.documentation:
Value: 9
Specifies the character's maximum deceleration rate (default is 30 m/s²).

@sl-slua/global/CHARACTER_MAX_SPEED.documentation:
Value: 13
Specifies the character's maximum speed (must be between 0.2 and 50 meters/second; default is 20 m/s). Affects speed when avoiding dynamic obstacles and when traversing low-walkability objects in TRAVERSAL_TYPE_FAST mode.

@sl-slua/global/CHARACTER_MAX_TURN_RADIU.documentation:
Value: 10
Specifies the character's turn radius when traveling at CHARACTER_DESIRED_TURN_SPEED (default is 1.25 meters).

@sl-slua/global/CHARACTER_ORIENTATION.documentation:
Value: 4
Specifies the orientation of the collision capsule for a pathfinding character to denote how to interpret its size. Valid options are VERTICAL and HORIZONTAL.

@sl-slua/global/CHARACTER_RADIUS.documentation:
Value: 2
Sets the collision capsule radius used to define the size of a pathfinding character.

@sl-slua/global/CHARACTER_STAY_WITHIN_PA.documentation:
Value: 15
Determines whether a character is restricted to its starting parcel (default is FALSE). If TRUE, parcel boundaries act as one-way obstacles; the character cannot voluntarily leave and pathfinding behaviors (wander, flee, evade, pursue) will only choose goal points within the parcel. If pushed out, it can re-enter but cannot voluntarily leave again.

@sl-slua/global/CHARACTER_TYPE.documentation:
Value: 6
Specifies the preferred surface and terrain terrain-type for the character (default is CHARACTER_TYPE_NONE). Used to describe preferred surfaces rather than behavior (e.g., cows prefer type B, sheep herded along a road prefer type C).

@sl-slua/global/CHARACTER_TYPE_A.documentation:
Value: 0
Used for pathfinding character types that prefer movement consistent with humanoids.

@sl-slua/global/CHARACTER_TYPE_B.documentation:
Value: 1
Used for pathfinding character types that prefer movement consistent with wild animals or off-road vehicles.

@sl-slua/global/CHARACTER_TYPE_C.documentation:
Value: 2
Used for mechanical pathfinding character types or road-going vehicles.

@sl-slua/global/CHARACTER_TYPE_D.documentation:
Value: 3
Used for pathfinding character types that are inconsistent with types A, B, or C.

@sl-slua/global/CHARACTER_TYPE_NONE.documentation:
Value: 4
Used to set no specific pathfinding character type.

@sl-slua/global/CLICK_ACTION_BUY.documentation:
Value: 2
Opens the buy dialog when the prim is clicked or touched.

@sl-slua/global/CLICK_ACTION_DISABLED.documentation:
Value: 8
Disables click actions. No touches are detected or passed.

@sl-slua/global/CLICK_ACTION_IGNORE.documentation:
Value: 9
Disables click actions. Clicks pass through the object to whatever is behind it, and no touches are detected.

@sl-slua/global/CLICK_ACTION_NONE.documentation:
Value: 0
Performs the default action: triggers touch events when the prim is clicked or touched.

@sl-slua/global/CLICK_ACTION_OPEN.documentation:
Value: 4
Opens the object inventory dialog when the prim is clicked or touched.

@sl-slua/global/CLICK_ACTION_OPEN_MEDIA.documentation:
Value: 6
Opens the web media dialog or plays parcel media (without pausing) when the prim is touched.

@sl-slua/global/CLICK_ACTION_PAY.documentation:
Value: 3
Opens the pay dialog when the prim is clicked or touched.

@sl-slua/global/CLICK_ACTION_PLAY.documentation:
Value: 5
Enables HTML-on-a-prim or plays/pauses parcel media when the prim is clicked or touched.

@sl-slua/global/CLICK_ACTION_SIT.documentation:
Value: 1
Causes the avatar to sit on the prim when it is clicked or touched.

@sl-slua/global/CLICK_ACTION_TOUCH.documentation:
Value: 0
Triggers touch events when the prim is clicked or touched.

@sl-slua/global/CLICK_ACTION_ZOOM.documentation:
Value: 7
Zooms the avatar camera in on the object (Viewer 2) when clicked or touched.

@sl-slua/global/COMBAT_CHANNEL.documentation:
Value: 2147483646
A region-wide channel reserved for combat-related log events. Passing this to llRegionSay adds the message to the combat log, and scripts listening on this channel can monitor the combat log.

@sl-slua/global/COMBAT_LOG_ID.documentation:
Value: uuid("45e0fcfa-2268-4490-a51c-3e51bdfe80d1")
The ID used by all combat log messages sent from the region to the COMBAT_CHANNEL. Scripts can filter llListen calls with this ID to receive only system-generated combat log messages.

@sl-slua/global/CONTENT_TYPE_ATOM.documentation:
Value: 4
Sets the 'Content-Type' header of subsequent LSL HTTP server responses via llHTTPResponse to 'application/atom+xml'.

@sl-slua/global/CONTENT_TYPE_FORM.documentation:
Value: 7
Sets the 'Content-Type' header of subsequent LSL HTTP server responses via llHTTPResponse to 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded'.

@sl-slua/global/CONTENT_TYPE_HTML.documentation:
Value: 1
Sets the 'Content-Type' header of subsequent LSL HTTP server responses via llHTTPResponse to 'text/html'. Only valid for embedded browsers on content owned by the viewer; falls back to 'text/plain' otherwise.

@sl-slua/global/CONTENT_TYPE_JSON.documentation:
Value: 5
Sets the 'Content-Type' header of subsequent LSL HTTP server responses via llHTTPResponse to 'application/json'.

@sl-slua/global/CONTENT_TYPE_LLSD.documentation:
Value: 6
Sets the 'Content-Type' header of subsequent LSL HTTP server responses via llHTTPResponse to 'application/llsd+xml' (Linden Lab Structured Data).

@sl-slua/global/CONTENT_TYPE_RSS.documentation:
Value: 8
Sets the 'Content-Type' header of subsequent LSL HTTP server responses via llHTTPResponse to 'application/rss+xml'.

@sl-slua/global/CONTENT_TYPE_TEXT.documentation:
Value: 0
Sets the 'Content-Type' header of subsequent LSL HTTP server responses via llHTTPResponse to 'text/plain'.

@sl-slua/global/CONTENT_TYPE_XHTML.documentation:
Value: 3
Sets the 'Content-Type' header of subsequent LSL HTTP server responses via llHTTPResponse to 'application/xhtml+xml'.

@sl-slua/global/CONTENT_TYPE_XML.documentation:
Value: 2
Sets the 'Content-Type' header of subsequent LSL HTTP server responses via llHTTPResponse to 'application/xml'.

@sl-slua/global/CONTROL_BACK.documentation:
Value: 0x2
Tests for the avatar move back control (↓ or S).

@sl-slua/global/CONTROL_DOWN.documentation:
Value: 0x20
Tests for the avatar move down control (PgDn or C).

@sl-slua/global/CONTROL_FWD.documentation:
Value: 0x1
Tests for the avatar move forward control (↑ or W).

@sl-slua/global/CONTROL_LBUTTON.documentation:
Value: 0x10000000
Tests for the avatar left mouse button control.

@sl-slua/global/CONTROL_LEFT.documentation:
Value: 0x4
Tests for the avatar move left control (Shift + ← or Shift + A).

@sl-slua/global/CONTROL_ML_LBUTTON.documentation:
Value: 0x40000000
Tests for the avatar left mouse button control while in mouselook.

@sl-slua/global/CONTROL_RIGHT.documentation:
Value: 0x8
Tests for the avatar move right control (Shift + → or Shift + D).

@sl-slua/global/CONTROL_ROT_LEFT.documentation:
Value: 0x100
Tests for the avatar rotate left control (← or A).

@sl-slua/global/CONTROL_ROT_RIGHT.documentation:
Value: 0x200
Tests for the avatar rotate right control (→ or D).

@sl-slua/global/CONTROL_UP.documentation:
Value: 0x10
Tests for the avatar move up control (PgUp or E).

@sl-slua/global/DAMAGEABLE.documentation:
Value: 0x20
Identifies objects in the world that can process damage. In llDetectedType, it indicates a damageable agent, or an object containing a script with on_damage or final_damage events. Can also filter llSensor/llSensorRepeat calls.

@sl-slua/global/DAMAGE_TYPE_ACID.documentation:
Value: 1
Damage caused by a caustic substance, such as acid.

@sl-slua/global/DAMAGE_TYPE_BLUDGEONING.documentation:
Value: 2
Damage caused by a blunt object, such as a club.

@sl-slua/global/DAMAGE_TYPE_COLD.documentation:
Value: 3
Damage inflicted by exposure to extreme cold.

@sl-slua/global/DAMAGE_TYPE_FIRE.documentation:
Value: 5
Damage inflicted by exposure to heat or flames.

@sl-slua/global/DAMAGE_TYPE_FORCE.documentation:
Value: 6
Damage inflicted by a great force or impact (Vertical Sim / SLMC).

@sl-slua/global/DAMAGE_TYPE_IMPACT.documentation:
Value: -1
System damage generated by impact with land, terrain, or a prim.

@sl-slua/global/DAMAGE_TYPE_NECROTIC.documentation:
Value: 7
Damage caused by a direct assault on the life-force.

@sl-slua/global/DAMAGE_TYPE_PIERCING.documentation:
Value: 8
Damage caused by a piercing object such as a bullet, spear, or arrow (Vertical Sim / SLMC).

@sl-slua/global/DAMAGE_TYPE_PSYCHIC.documentation:
Value: 10
Damage caused by a direct assault on the mind.

@sl-slua/global/DAMAGE_TYPE_RADIANT.documentation:
Value: 11
Damage caused by radiation or extreme light.

@sl-slua/global/DAMAGE_TYPE_SLASHING.documentation:
Value: 12
Damage caused by a slashing object such as a sword or axe.

@sl-slua/global/DAMAGE_TYPE_SONIC.documentation:
Value: 13
Damage caused by loud noises, like a Crash Worship concert.

@sl-slua/global/DATA_BORN.documentation:
Value: 3
Used with llRequestAgentData to return the agent's account creation ('born on') date as a string in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD), based on Pacific Time (not UTC).

@sl-slua/global/DATA_NAME.documentation:
Value: 2
Used with llRequestAgentData to return the requested agent's legacy name.

@sl-slua/global/DATA_ONLINE.documentation:
Value: 1
Used with llRequestAgentData to return whether the requested agent is online (returns an integer boolean string: TRUE if online, FALSE if offline).

@sl-slua/global/DATA_PAYINFO.documentation:
Value: 8
Used with llRequestAgentData to return a string containing the integer mask flag for payment status (contains PAYMENT_INFO_ON_FILE, PAYMENT_INFO_USED, or both).

@sl-slua/global/DATA_RATING.documentation:
Value: 4
Deprecated. Used with llRequestAgentData to return the string '0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0'. It formerly returned a comma-separated list of positive and negative ratings (behavior, appearance, and building) before ratings were removed from SL.

@sl-slua/global/DATA_SIM_POS.documentation:
Value: 5
Used with llRequestSimulatorData to return the region's global position as a vector.

@sl-slua/global/DATA_SIM_RATING.documentation:
Value: 7
Used with llRequestSimulatorData to return the simulator rating string ('PG', 'MATURE', 'ADULT', or 'UNKNOWN').

@sl-slua/global/DATA_SIM_STATUS.documentation:
Value: 6
Used with llRequestSimulatorData to return the simulator status as a string.

@sl-slua/global/DEBUG_CHANNEL.documentation:
Value: 2147483647
A chat channel reserved for script debugging and error messages. Passing this to llSay, llWhisper, or llShout prints text to the Script Warning/Error Window and broadcasts it to nearby avatars' script consoles.

@sl-slua/global/DEG_TO_RAD.documentation:
Value: 0.017453293
The constant 0.017453293 (precise value is PI/180). Multiply a value in degrees by this number to convert it to radians.

@sl-slua/global/DENSITY.documentation:
Value: 1
Used with llSetPhysicsMaterial to indicate that the density parameter is enabled (which overrides the previous value). Must be between 1.0 and 22587.0 kg/m³.

@sl-slua/global/DEREZ_DIE.documentation:
Value: 0
Used with llDerezObject to immediately delete (kill) the object.

@sl-slua/global/DEREZ_MAKE_TEMP.documentation:
Value: 1
Used with llDerezObject to mark the object as temporary, allowing the simulator to clean it up at a later time.

@sl-slua/global/DEREZ_TO_INVENTORY.documentation:
Value: 2
Used with llDerezObject to return the targeted object to the rezzer's (derezzer's) inventory, saving its current state.

@sl-slua/global/ENVIRONMENT_DAYINFO.documentation:
Value: 200
Day cycle and time information: day_length (seconds in the environment's day cycle), day_offset (seconds offset from GMT), and secs_since_midnight (seconds elapsed since the last day cycle midnight).

@sl-slua/global/ENV_INVALID_AGENT.documentation:
Value: -4
Unable to find the specified agent, or could not find an agent with the specified ID.

@sl-slua/global/ENV_INVALID_RULE.documentation:
Value: -5
There was an issue with one of the rules, or an attempt was made to change an unknown property.

@sl-slua/global/ENV_NOT_EXPERIENCE.documentation:
Value: -1
Attempted to change environments outside an experience, or the script is not running as part of an experience with a valid experience key.

@sl-slua/global/ENV_NO_ENVIRONMENT.documentation:
Value: -3
The environmental settings inventory object could not be found, or there is no environment on this parcel to modify.

@sl-slua/global/ENV_NO_EXPERIENCE_LAND.documentation:
Value: -7
The experience has not been enabled or cannot run on the land.

@sl-slua/global/ENV_NO_EXPERIENCE_PERMIS.documentation:
Value: -2
The agent has not granted permission to change environments.

@sl-slua/global/ENV_NO_PERMISSIONS.documentation:
Value: -9
The script lacks permissions to modify the environment at this location, or an attempt was made to remove altitude track 0 or 1.

@sl-slua/global/ENV_THROTTLE.documentation:
Value: -8
The scripts have exceeded the throttle limit. Wait and retry the request.

@sl-slua/global/ENV_VALIDATION_FAIL.documentation:
Value: -6
Could not validate the environmental settings or values passed.

@sl-slua/global/EOF.documentation:
Value: " "
A value equal to three newline characters ("


") returned by the dataserver event, indicating that the requested line is past the end of the notecard.

@sl-slua/global/ERR_GENERIC.documentation:
Value: -1
An inexplicable and generic error where nothing is known about the cause.

@sl-slua/global/ERR_MALFORMED_PARAMS.documentation:
Value: -3
Return value for llReturnObject* functions indicating that the parameters passed are malformed.

@sl-slua/global/ERR_PARCEL_PERMISSIONS.documentation:
Value: -2
Return value for llReturnObject* functions indicating a lack of permissions to perform the task on the specified parcel.

@sl-slua/global/ERR_RUNTIME_PERMISSIONS.documentation:
Value: -4
Return value for llReturnObject* functions indicating the script lacks the runtime permissions required to perform the requested task.

@sl-slua/global/ERR_THROTTLED.documentation:
Value: -5
Return value for llReturnObject* functions indicating that the task has been throttled and should be retried later.

@sl-slua/global/ESTATE_ACCESS_ALLOWED_AG.documentation:
Value: 4
Used as an input parameter for llManageEstateAccess to add an agent to the estate's Allowed Residents list.

Value: 8
Used as an input parameter for llManageEstateAccess to remove an agent from the estate's Allowed Residents list.

@sl-slua/global/ESTATE_ACCESS_ALLOWED_GR.documentation:
Value: 16
Used as an input parameter for llManageEstateAccess to add a group to the estate's Allowed Groups list.

Value: 32
Used as an input parameter for llManageEstateAccess to remove a group from the estate's Allowed Groups list.

@sl-slua/global/ESTATE_ACCESS_BANNED_AGE.documentation:
Value: 64
Used as an input parameter for llManageEstateAccess to add an agent to the estate's Banned Residents list.

Value: 128
Used as an input parameter for llManageEstateAccess to remove an agent from the estate's Banned Residents list.

@sl-slua/global/FILTER_FLAGS.documentation:
Value: 2
Flags used to control which attachments are returned.

@sl-slua/global/FILTER_FLAG_HUDS.documentation:
Value: 0x0001
Filter flag used with llGetAttachedListFiltered to include HUDs with matching experiences in the returned result.

@sl-slua/global/FILTER_INCLUDE.documentation:
Value: 1
Filter parameter used to include a specific attachment point.

@sl-slua/global/FORCE_DIRECT_PATH.documentation:
Value: 1
Forces the pathfinding character to navigate in a straight line toward the specified position (can be set to TRUE or FALSE).

@sl-slua/global/FRICTION.documentation:
Value: 2
Used with llSetPhysicsMaterial to enable the friction override. The value must be between 0.0 and 255.0.

@sl-slua/global/GCNP_RADIUS.documentation:
Value: 0
Used with llGetClosestNavPoint to limit how far out to search for a navigation point.

@sl-slua/global/GCNP_STATIC.documentation:
Value: 1
Used with llGetClosestNavPoint to specify that the test should use the static navmesh, ignoring all dynamic obstacles.

@sl-slua/global/GRAVITY_MULTIPLIER.documentation:
Value: 8
Used with llSetPhysicsMaterial to enable the gravity multiplier override. The value must be between -1.0 and +28.0.

@sl-slua/global/HORIZONTAL.documentation:
Value: 1
Constant indicating that the collision capsule orientation for a pathfinding character is horizontal.

@sl-slua/global/HTTP_ACCEPT.documentation:
Value: 8
Used with llHTTPRequest to override the default Accept header with custom MIME types (such as application/json, text/html, etc.). Responses with unlisted content types will return status 415.

@sl-slua/global/HTTP_BODY_MAXLENGTH.documentation:
Value: 2
Sets the maximum (UTF-8 encoded) byte length of the HTTP response body. The maximum limit depends on the VM being used (Mono Max: 16384 bytes, LSO Max: 4096 bytes).

@sl-slua/global/HTTP_BODY_TRUNCATED.documentation:
Value: 0
Indicates the response body truncation point in bytes.

@sl-slua/global/HTTP_CUSTOM_HEADER.documentation:
Value: 5
Adds a custom HTTP header (name and value pair) to the request. Up to 8 custom headers can be configured per request. Some default or sensitive headers are blocked for security reasons.

@sl-slua/global/HTTP_EXTENDED_ERROR.documentation:
Value: 9
If set to TRUE, llHTTPRequest always returns a key. If an error occurs during the request, detailed error information (delivered in an RFC 7807 JSON block) is reported through the http_response event using that key.

@sl-slua/global/HTTP_METHOD.documentation:
Value: 0
Specifies the HTTP method ('GET', 'POST', 'PUT', or 'DELETE') for the request.

@sl-slua/global/HTTP_MIMETYPE.documentation:
Value: 1
Specifies the request Content-Type MIME type. Text types should define a charset (e.g., 'text/plain;charset=utf-8'). Use 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded' to emulate HTML forms.

@sl-slua/global/HTTP_PRAGMA_NO_CACHE.documentation:
Value: 6
Controls whether the 'Pragma: no-cache' header is sent with the HTTP request. When SendHeader is TRUE (default), the header is sent; when FALSE, it is omitted.

@sl-slua/global/HTTP_USER_AGENT.documentation:
Value: 7
Appends a custom string to the default LSL User-Agent header value. Must follow HTTP standard syntax (e.g., 'My-Script-Name/1.0'). Spaces are not allowed and will cause a script error; use hyphens instead.

@sl-slua/global/HTTP_VERBOSE_THROTTLE.documentation:
Value: 4
If set to TRUE, shouts error messages to DEBUG_CHANNEL if the outgoing request rate exceeds the server limit. If FALSE, these error messages are suppressed.

@sl-slua/global/HTTP_VERIFY_CERT.documentation:
Value: 3
If set to TRUE, the server's SSL certificate must be verifiable using a standard certificate authority when making HTTPS requests. If FALSE, any SSL certificate is accepted.

@sl-slua/global/INVENTORY_ALL.documentation:
Value: -1
Used with inventory functions to specify that all types of inventory items should be retrieved.

@sl-slua/global/INVENTORY_ANIMATION.documentation:
Value: 20
Used with inventory functions to filter or retrieve items of the ANIMATION type.

@sl-slua/global/INVENTORY_BODYPART.documentation:
Value: 13
Used with inventory functions to filter or retrieve items of the BODYPART type.

@sl-slua/global/INVENTORY_CLOTHING.documentation:
Value: 5
Used with inventory functions to filter or retrieve items of the CLOTHING type.

@sl-slua/global/INVENTORY_GESTURE.documentation:
Value: 21
Used with inventory functions to filter or retrieve items of the GESTURE type.

@sl-slua/global/INVENTORY_LANDMARK.documentation:
Value: 3
Used with inventory functions to filter or retrieve items of the LANDMARK type.

@sl-slua/global/INVENTORY_MATERIAL.documentation:
Value: 57
Used with inventory functions to filter or retrieve items of the MATERIAL type.

@sl-slua/global/INVENTORY_NONE.documentation:
Value: -1
Value returned by inventory functions indicating that the specified inventory item does not exist.

@sl-slua/global/INVENTORY_NOTECARD.documentation:
Value: 7
Used with inventory functions to filter or retrieve items of the NOTECARD type.

@sl-slua/global/INVENTORY_OBJECT.documentation:
Value: 6
Used with inventory functions to filter or retrieve items of the OBJECT type.

@sl-slua/global/INVENTORY_SCRIPT.documentation:
Value: 10
Used with inventory functions to filter or retrieve items of the SCRIPT type.

@sl-slua/global/INVENTORY_SETTING.documentation:
Value: 56
Used with inventory functions to filter or retrieve items of the SETTING type.

@sl-slua/global/INVENTORY_SOUND.documentation:
Value: 1
Used with inventory functions to filter or retrieve items of the SOUND type.

@sl-slua/global/INVENTORY_TEXTURE.documentation:
Value: 0
Used with inventory functions to filter or retrieve items of the TEXTURE type.

@sl-slua/global/JSON_APPEND.documentation:
Value: -1
A specifier for llJsonSetValue indicating the given value should be appended to the array at the specified level. If the target value is not an array, the existing data will be overwritten and replaced by the new array.

@sl-slua/global/JSON_ARRAY.documentation:
Value: "\u{FDD2}"
Used with llList2Json to indicate that the provided list should be encoded and returned as a string-serialized JSON array.

@sl-slua/global/JSON_DELETE.documentation:
Value: "\u{FDD8}"
A constant used to delete a value within a JSON text string.

@sl-slua/global/JSON_FALSE.documentation:
Value: "\u{FDD7}"
Return value for llJsonValueType indicating that the value at the specified address in a JSON string is a boolean FALSE.

@sl-slua/global/JSON_INVALID.documentation:
Value: "\u{FDD0}"
Indicates an invalid state or parameter. Returned by llList2Json when inputs are not well-formed, by llJsonValueType for an invalid JSON type, or by llJsonGetValue when attempting to access a nonexistent location.

@sl-slua/global/JSON_NULL.documentation:
Value: "\u{FDD5}"
Return value for llJsonValueType indicating that the value at the specified address in a JSON string is a JSON null.

@sl-slua/global/JSON_NUMBER.documentation:
Value: "\u{FDD3}"
Return value for llJsonValueType indicating that the value at the specified address in a JSON string is a number.

@sl-slua/global/JSON_OBJECT.documentation:
Value: "\u{FDD1}"
Used with llList2Json to indicate that the provided list is a strided list of key-value pairs to be encoded and returned as a JSON object.

@sl-slua/global/JSON_STRING.documentation:
Value: "\u{FDD4}"
Return value for llJsonValueType indicating that the value at the specified address in a JSON string is a string.

@sl-slua/global/JSON_TRUE.documentation:
Value: "\u{FDD6}"
Return value for llJsonValueType indicating that the value at the specified address in a JSON string is a boolean TRUE.

@sl-slua/global/KFM_CMD_PAUSE.documentation:
Value: 2
Command used with KFM_COMMAND in llSetKeyframedMotion to pause the keyframed motion without resetting it to the start.

@sl-slua/global/KFM_CMD_PLAY.documentation:
Value: 0
Command used with KFM_COMMAND in llSetKeyframedMotion to resume a motion previously stopped by KFM_CMD_STOP or paused by KFM_CMD_PAUSE.

@sl-slua/global/KFM_CMD_STOP.documentation:
Value: 1
Command used with KFM_COMMAND in llSetKeyframedMotion to stop the keyframed motion and reset it to the start.

@sl-slua/global/KFM_COMMAND.documentation:
Value: 0
Option flag in llSetKeyframedMotion followed by KFM_CMD_STOP, KFM_CMD_PLAY, or KFM_CMD_PAUSE to play, stop, or pause the motion.

@sl-slua/global/KFM_DATA.documentation:
Value: 2
Option flag in llSetKeyframedMotion followed by a bitwise combination of KFM_TRANSLATION and KFM_ROTATION to specify keyframe data. If absent, both rotation and translation data must be provided.

@sl-slua/global/KFM_FORWARD.documentation:
Value: 0
Playback mode used with KFM_MODE in llSetKeyframedMotion (default). It plays the frames sequentially from 1 to N and then stops.

@sl-slua/global/KFM_LOOP.documentation:
Value: 1
Playback mode used with KFM_MODE in llSetKeyframedMotion. It plays the frames sequentially from 1 to N, returns to the initial position, and repeats indefinitely.

@sl-slua/global/KFM_MODE.documentation:
Value: 1
Option flag in llSetKeyframedMotion followed by KFM_LOOP, KFM_REVERSE, KFM_FORWARD, or KFM_PING_PONG to set the playback mode (defaults to KFM_FORWARD). Must be specified when the keyframe list is provided.

@sl-slua/global/KFM_PING_PONG.documentation:
Value: 2
Playback mode used with KFM_MODE in llSetKeyframedMotion.

@sl-slua/global/KFM_REVERSE.documentation:
Value: 3
Playback mode used with KFM_MODE in llSetKeyframedMotion. It plays the frames in reverse order (from N down to 1) and then stops.

@sl-slua/global/KFM_ROTATION.documentation:
Value: 1
Specifies that rotation data is included in the moves list for llSetKeyframedMotion.

@sl-slua/global/KFM_TRANSLATION.documentation:
Value: 2
Specifies that translation data is included in the moves list for llSetKeyframedMotion.

@sl-slua/global/LAND_BRUSH_LARGE.documentation:
Value: 2
Use a large brush size (8m x 8m).

@sl-slua/global/LAND_BRUSH_MEDIUM.documentation:
Value: 1
Use a medium brush size (4m x 4m)..

@sl-slua/global/LAND_BRUSH_SMALL.documentation:
Value: 0
Use a small brush size (2m x 2m).

@sl-slua/global/LAND_LARGE_BRUSH.documentation:
Value: 3
Legacy large brush size (8m x 8m). Note: This constant value is incorrect; use LAND_BRUSH_LARGE instead.

@sl-slua/global/LAND_LEVEL.documentation:
Value: 0
Action used with llModifyLand to level the land at the prim's center.

@sl-slua/global/LAND_LOWER.documentation:
Value: 2
Action used with llModifyLand to lower the land.

@sl-slua/global/LAND_MEDIUM_BRUSH.documentation:
Value: 2
Legacy medium brush size (4m x 4m). Note: This constant value is incorrect; use LAND_BRUSH_MEDIUM instead.

@sl-slua/global/LAND_NOISE.documentation:
Value: 4
Action used with llModifyLand to randomize the terrain and make it rough.

@sl-slua/global/LAND_RAISE.documentation:
Value: 1
Action used with llModifyLand to raise the land.

@sl-slua/global/LAND_REVERT.documentation:
Value: 5
Action used with llModifyLand to restore the land to its baked value.

@sl-slua/global/LAND_SMALL_BRUSH.documentation:
Value: 1
Legacy small brush size (2m x 2m). Note: This constant value is incorrect; use LAND_BRUSH_SMALL instead.

@sl-slua/global/LAND_SMOOTH.documentation:
Value: 3
Action used with llModifyLand to smooth the land.

@sl-slua/global/LINKSETDATA_DELETE.documentation:
Value: 2
Event or flag indicating a key-value pair has been removed from the linkset datastore, either via llLinksetDataDelete or by writing an empty value.

@sl-slua/global/LINKSETDATA_EMEMORY.documentation:
Value: 1
Error code indicating a key-value pair was too large to write to the linkset datastore.

@sl-slua/global/LINKSETDATA_ENOKEY.documentation:
Value: 2
Error code indicating the key name supplied to a linkset datastore operation was empty.

@sl-slua/global/LINKSETDATA_EPROTECTED.documentation:
Value: 3
Error code indicating the key-value pair is protected from being deleted or overwritten in the linkset datastore.

@sl-slua/global/LINKSETDATA_MULTIDELETE.documentation:
Value: 3
Flag or value indicating a comma-separated list of keys has been deleted from the linkset datastore via llLinksetDataDeleteFound.

@sl-slua/global/LINKSETDATA_NOTFOUND.documentation:
Value: 4
Error code indicating the specified key could not be found in the linkset datastore.

@sl-slua/global/LINKSETDATA_NOUPDATE.documentation:
Value: 5
Status code indicating that the linkset datastore was not updated because the written value matched the existing stored value.

@sl-slua/global/LINKSETDATA_OK.documentation:
Value: 0
Status code indicating the linkset datastore operation completed successfully.

@sl-slua/global/LINKSETDATA_RESET.documentation:
Value: 0
Event or flag indicating the linkset datastore has been cleared/reset via llLinksetDataReset.

@sl-slua/global/LINKSETDATA_UPDATE.documentation:
Value: 1
Event or flag indicating a key in the linkset datastore has been created or updated with a new value via llLinksetDataWrite.

@sl-slua/global/LINK_ALL_CHILDREN.documentation:
Value: -3
Refers to all child prims in the linkset (every prim except the root).

@sl-slua/global/LINK_ALL_OTHERS.documentation:
Value: -2
Refers to every other prim in the linkset, excluding the prim containing the script.

@sl-slua/global/LINK_ROOT.documentation:
Value: 1
Refers to the root prim of the linkset.

@sl-slua/global/LINK_SET.documentation:
Value: -1
Refers to every prim in the linkset.

@sl-slua/global/LINK_THIS.documentation:
Value: -4
Refers specifically to the single prim containing the running script.

@sl-slua/global/LIST_STAT_GEOMETRIC_MEAN.documentation:
Value: 9
Option used with llListStatistics to calculate the geometric mean of a list of numbers.

@sl-slua/global/LIST_STAT_MAX.documentation:
Value: 2
Option used with llListStatistics to find the largest number in the list.

@sl-slua/global/LIST_STAT_MEAN.documentation:
Value: 3
Option used with llListStatistics to calculate the mean (average) of the numbers in the list.

@sl-slua/global/LIST_STAT_MEDIAN.documentation:
Value: 4
Option used with llListStatistics to calculate the median of the numbers in the list.

@sl-slua/global/LIST_STAT_MIN.documentation:
Value: 1
Option used with llListStatistics to find the smallest number in the list.

@sl-slua/global/LIST_STAT_NUM_COUNT.documentation:
Value: 8
Option used with llListStatistics to determine the count of float and integer elements in the list.

@sl-slua/global/LIST_STAT_RANGE.documentation:
Value: 0
Option used with llListStatistics to calculate the range (maximum value minus minimum value) of the list.

@sl-slua/global/LIST_STAT_STD_DEV.documentation:
Value: 5
Option used with llListStatistics to calculate the sample standard deviation of a list of numbers.

@sl-slua/global/LIST_STAT_SUM.documentation:
Value: 6
Option used with llListStatistics to calculate the sum of the numbers in the list.

@sl-slua/global/LIST_STAT_SUM_SQUARES.documentation:
Value: 7
Option used with llListStatistics to calculate the sum of the squares of the numbers in the list.

@sl-slua/global/LOOP.documentation:
Value: 0x2
Used with texture animation functions to cause the animation to loop continuously.

@sl-slua/global/MASK_BASE.documentation:
Value: 0
Permissions mask option representing base permissions.

@sl-slua/global/MASK_COMBINED.documentation:
Value: 0x10
Permissions mask option to include the object's inventory contents when calculating permissions. Can be combined with other mask flags (e.g., MASK_OWNER | MASK_COMBINED).

@sl-slua/global/MASK_EVERYONE.documentation:
Value: 3
Permissions mask option representing permissions held by everyone.

@sl-slua/global/MASK_GROUP.documentation:
Value: 2
Permissions mask option representing active group permissions.

@sl-slua/global/MASK_NEXT.documentation:
Value: 4
Permissions mask option representing permissions the next owner will inherit.

@sl-slua/global/MASK_OWNER.documentation:
Value: 1
Permissions mask option representing current owner permissions.

@sl-slua/global/NAK.documentation:
Value: "\n\x15\n"
Value returned by llGetNotecardLineSync (ASCII NAK surrounded by newlines, i.e., codes 10, 21, 10) when requested notecard data is not in the region's cache.

@sl-slua/global/NULL_KEY.documentation:
Value: uuid("00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000")
A special string constant used as a null or empty key value.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_ACCOUNT_LEVEL.documentation:
Value: 41
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to retrieve an avatar's account level. Returns 0 for Basic, 1 for Premium, 5 for Plus, 10 for Premium Plus, or -1 if the target is not an avatar.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_ANIMATED_COUNT.documentation:
Value: 39
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails. For an object, returns a boolean indicating if the root is set as 'Animated Mesh'. For an agent, returns the total number of 'Animated Mesh' attachments worn.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_ANIMATED_SLOTS_AV.documentation:
Value: 40
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the avatar's available 'Animated Mesh' attachment slot count (returns 0 if the ID is not an avatar).

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_ATTACHED_POINT.documentation:
Value: 19
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails. Returns the integer attachment point matching an ATTACH_* constant, or 0 if the target is not an attachment (e.g., an avatar or unattached object).

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_ATTACHED_SLOTS_AV.documentation:
Value: 35
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get an avatar's available attachment slot count. Returns 0 if the ID is not an avatar or if no slots are available.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_BODY_SHAPE_TYPE.documentation:
Value: 26
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to retrieve a float representing the gender/sex setting of an avatar's shape (0.0 is standard female, 1.0 is standard male, and values greater than or equal to 0.5 are considered male). Returns -1.0 if not an avatar or if no data is available.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_CHARACTER_TIME.documentation:
Value: 17
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the average CPU time (in seconds) spent on navigation if the target is a pathfinding character. Returns 0 for non-characters.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_CLICK_ACTION.documentation:
Value: 28
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the click action of a prim. Returns an integer corresponding to a CLICK_ACTION_* constant (default is 0).

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_CREATION_TIME.documentation:
Value: 36
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the object's creation time (established via build-menu rezzing or mesh uploads, not via inventory/script rezzes, copying, or modifying). Returns an empty string for avatars.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_CREATOR.documentation:
Value: 8
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the key of the prim's creator. Returns NULL_KEY if the ID is an avatar.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_DAMAGE.documentation:
Value: 51
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to retrieve the amount of damage a prim inflicts upon collision.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_DAMAGE_TYPE.documentation:
Value: 52
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to retrieve the type of damage a prim inflicts on collision. Returns an integer matching a DAMAGE_TYPE_* constant, a custom type, or a value repurposed by a combat system.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_DESC.documentation:
Value: 2
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to retrieve the prim's description. Returns an empty string if the ID is an avatar.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_GROUP.documentation:
Value: 7
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the prim's group key. Returns NULL_KEY if the target is an avatar (which means a workaround is required to get an avatar's active group).

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_GROUP_TAG.documentation:
Value: 33
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get an avatar's active group tag/title. Returns an empty string if the target is not an avatar.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_HEALTH.documentation:
Value: 50
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to retrieve the current health value of an avatar or prim.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_HOVER_HEIGHT.documentation:
Value: 25
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the dynamic hover height of an avatar (default is 0.0). Returns 0.0 if not an avatar or if no data is available; does not reflect the shape's 'Hover' slider.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_LAST_OWNER_ID.documentation:
Value: 27
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the key of the previous owner. For group-owned objects, returns the avatar who deeded it. Returns NULL_KEY for avatars, untransferred objects, or objects taken to inventory and re-rezzed.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_LINK_NUMBER.documentation:
Value: 46
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the index of the prim within its linkset, or 0 if unlinked.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_LOCKED.documentation:
Value: 55
Returns boolean, indicating if object is locked.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_MASS.documentation:
Value: 43
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to retrieve the total mass (in kilograms) of the object's linkset.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_MATERIAL.documentation:
Value: 42
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to retrieve the physics material of the object, returning an integer matching a PRIM_MATERIAL_* constant.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_NAME.documentation:
Value: 1
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the prim's name, or the legacy name if the target is an avatar.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_OMEGA.documentation:
Value: 29
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the rotational (angular) velocity of an object in radians per second.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_OWNER.documentation:
Value: 6
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the owner's key. If the target is an avatar, their own key is returned. If group-owned, it returns NULL_KEY.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_PATHFINDING_TYPE.documentation:
Value: 20
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the pathfinding setting of an object in the region, returning an integer matching an OPT_* constant.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_PERMS.documentation:
Value: 53
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to retrieve the permissions of the object as five integers.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_PERMS_COMBINED.documentation:
Value: 54
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to retrieve the permissions of the object combined with all of its inventory items as five integers.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_PHANTOM.documentation:
Value: 22
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get a boolean indicating if phantom is enabled on the object. Returns 0 if the target is an avatar or attachment.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_PHYSICS.documentation:
Value: 21
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get a boolean indicating if physics is enabled on the object. Returns 0 if the target is an avatar or attachment.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_PHYSICS_COST.documentation:
Value: 16
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the physics cost of the object.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_POS.documentation:
Value: 3
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the prim's position in region coordinates. For an avatar outside the region, the returned position is relative to the script's region.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_PRIM_COUNT.documentation:
Value: 30
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the prim count of the object. Note that the script and target object must be owned by the same owner.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_PRIM_EQUIVALENCE.documentation:
Value: 13
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the prim equivalence of the object.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_RENDER_WEIGHT.documentation:
Value: 24
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the render weight (Avatar Rendering Cost) of an avatar based on viewer reports. Returns 0 for objects, -1 if unknown, and is capped at 500,000.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_RETURN_PARCEL.documentation:
Value: 1
Scope flag used with llReturnObjectsByOwner to return all objects on the same parcel as the script owned by the specified owner. Requires the script owner to be an estate manager or the parcel owner.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_RETURN_PARCEL_OWN.documentation:
Value: 2
Scope flag used with llReturnObjectsByOwner to return all objects owned by the specified owner that are located on parcels owned by the script's owner.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_RETURN_REGION.documentation:
Value: 4
Scope flag used with llReturnObjectsByOwner to return all objects in the region owned by the specified owner. Only works if the script is owned by the estate owner or an estate manager.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_REZZER_KEY.documentation:
Value: 32
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to retrieve the key of the object or avatar that rezzed the target object.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_REZ_TIME.documentation:
Value: 45
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to retrieve the timestamp of when the object was rezzed.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_ROOT.documentation:
Value: 18
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the ID of the root prim. For an avatar, returns the root prim of the linkset they are sitting on (or the avatar's own ID if not sitting).

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_ROT.documentation:
Value: 4
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the prim's rotation.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_RUNNING_SCRIPT_CO.documentation:
Value: 9
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the total number of running scripts attached to the target object or agent.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_SCALE.documentation:
Value: 47
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the size (scale) of the object.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_SCRIPT_MEMORY.documentation:
Value: 11
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the allocated script memory (or its upper limit) in bytes for the object or agent. This value is recomputed every 10-20 seconds.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_SCRIPT_TIME.documentation:
Value: 12
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the average script CPU time (in seconds) used per frame. Reports a 30-minute average, or the average since entering the region if present for less than 30 minutes.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_SELECT_COUNT.documentation:
Value: 37
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the total number of agents/avatars actively selecting any link in the object. Returns 0 if the target is an avatar.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_SERVER_COST.documentation:
Value: 14
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the server cost of the object.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_SIT_COUNT.documentation:
Value: 38
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the total number of agents sitting on any links in the object. Returns 0 if the target is an avatar.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_STREAMING_COST.documentation:
Value: 15
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the streaming (download) cost of the object.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_TEMP_ATTACHED.documentation:
Value: 34
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get a boolean indicating whether the object is temporarily attached.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_TEMP_ON_REZ.documentation:
Value: 23
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get a boolean indicating if temporary-on-rez is enabled for the object.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_TEXT.documentation:
Value: 44
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the floating (hover) text displayed above the object.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_TEXT_ALPHA.documentation:
Value: 49
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the alpha value of the floating (hover) text displayed above the object.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_TEXT_COLOR.documentation:
Value: 48
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the color vector of the floating (hover) text displayed above the object.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_TOTAL_INVENTORY_C.documentation:
Value: 31
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the total number of inventory items inside the object. Requires the script and target object to share the same owner.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_TOTAL_SCRIPT_COUN.documentation:
Value: 10
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the total number of scripts (both running and stopped) attached to the target object or agent.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_UNKNOWN_DETAIL.documentation:
Value: -1
Flag returned by llGetObjectDetails when an invalid flag is requested.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_VELOCITY.documentation:
Value: 5
Flag used with llGetObjectDetails to get the velocity vector of the target object.

@sl-slua/global/OBJECT_VOLUME_DETECT.documentation:
Value: 56
Returns boolean, indicating if object is VolumeDetect.

@sl-slua/global/OPT_LEGACY_LINKSET.documentation:
Value: 0
Returned for movable obstacles, movable phantoms, physical, and volumedetect objects.

@sl-slua/global/OPT_OTHER.documentation:
Value: -1
Returned for attachments, Linden trees, and grass.

@sl-slua/global/OVERRIDE_GLTF_BASE_ALPHA.documentation:
Value: 2
GLTF override setting used with llSetLinkGLTFOverrides to set the alpha for the face(s). Only impacts rendering when the alpha mode is set to PRIM_GLTF_ALPHA_MODE_BLEND.

Value: 4
GLTF override setting used with llSetLinkGLTFOverrides to set the alpha cutoff level on the face(s) when the alpha mode is set to mask.

Value: 3
GLTF override setting used with llSetLinkGLTFOverrides to set the alpha mode on the face(s) to one of the valid blend modes.

@sl-slua/global/OVERRIDE_GLTF_BASE_COLOR.documentation:
Value: 1
GLTF override setting used with llSetLinkGLTFOverrides to set the tinting color (specified in linear RGB) used for the base color. Use llsRGB2Linear to convert colors from Blinn-Phong to PBR.

@sl-slua/global/OVERRIDE_GLTF_BASE_DOUBL.documentation:
Value: 5
GLTF override setting used with llSetLinkGLTFOverrides. If set to TRUE, the texture on the face(s) will be rendered as double-sided.

@sl-slua/global/OVERRIDE_GLTF_EMISSIVE_F.documentation:
Value: 8
GLTF override setting used with llSetLinkGLTFOverrides to set the tint (specified in linear RGB) used for the emissive texture on the face(s).

@sl-slua/global/OVERRIDE_GLTF_METALLIC_F.documentation:
Value: 6
GLTF override setting used with llSetLinkGLTFOverrides to adjust the metallic factor on the specified face(s) to a value between 0 and 1.

@sl-slua/global/OVERRIDE_GLTF_ROUGHNESS_.documentation:
Value: 7
GLTF override setting used with llSetLinkGLTFOverrides to adjust the roughness factor on the specified face(s) to a value between 0 and 1.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_COUNT_GROUP.documentation:
Value: 2
Used with llGetParcelPrimCount to get the total land impact of objects not owned by the parcel owner but set to or owned by the parcel's group.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_COUNT_OTHER.documentation:
Value: 3
Used with llGetParcelPrimCount to get the land impact of all objects that are neither owned by the parcel owner nor set to or owned by the parcel's group.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_COUNT_OWNER.documentation:
Value: 1
Used with llGetParcelPrimCount to get the total land impact of objects owned by the parcel owner.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_COUNT_SELECTED.documentation:
Value: 4
Used with llGetParcelPrimCount to get the total land impact of all objects currently selected or sat on.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_COUNT_TEMP.documentation:
Value: 5
Used with llGetParcelPrimCount to get the total land impact of temporary (temp-on-rez) objects.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_COUNT_TOTAL.documentation:
Value: 0
Used with llGetParcelPrimCount to get the total land impact of all objects on the parcel(s), excluding temporary (temp-on-rez) objects.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_DETAILS_AREA.documentation:
Value: 4
Flag used with llGetParcelDetails to retrieve the parcel's area in square meters (sqm).

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_DETAILS_DESC.documentation:
Value: 1
Flag used with llGetParcelDetails to retrieve the description of the parcel (up to 127 characters).

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_DETAILS_FLAGS.documentation:
Value: 12
Flag used with llGetParcelDetails to retrieve the parcel flags set on this parcel (see llGetParcelFlags for listings and meanings).

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_DETAILS_GROUP.documentation:
Value: 3
Flag used with llGetParcelDetails to retrieve the parcel group's 36-character key.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_DETAILS_ID.documentation:
Value: 5
Flag used with llGetParcelDetails to retrieve the parcel's 36-character UUID/key.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_DETAILS_LANDING_L.documentation:
Value: 10
Flag used with llGetParcelDetails to retrieve the lookat vector set for the landing point (teleport routing) on this parcel, if any.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_DETAILS_LANDING_P.documentation:
Value: 9
Flag used with llGetParcelDetails to retrieve the landing point vector set for this parcel, if any.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_DETAILS_NAME.documentation:
Value: 0
Flag used with llGetParcelDetails to retrieve the name of the parcel (up to 63 characters).

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_DETAILS_OWNER.documentation:
Value: 2
Flag used with llGetParcelDetails to retrieve the parcel owner's 36-character key.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_DETAILS_PRIM_CAPA.documentation:
Value: 7
Flag used with llGetParcelDetails to retrieve the total prim capacity on this and all same-owner parcels, sim-wide (see llGetParcelMaxPrims for same-parcel-only and/or sim-wide reporting).

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_DETAILS_PRIM_USED.documentation:
Value: 8
Flag used with llGetParcelDetails to retrieve the total prim usage on this and all same-owner parcels, sim-wide (see llGetParcelPrimCount to get prim counts by owner, group, or temporary status).

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_DETAILS_SCRIPT_DA.documentation:
Value: 13
Flag used with llGetParcelDetails to check if the script is in danger (due to restrictions that impact script execution) on the indicated parcel; see llScriptDanger.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_DETAILS_SEE_AVATA.documentation:
Value: 6
Flag used with llGetParcelDetails to retrieve the 'See and chat with residents on this parcel' avatar visibility setting.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_DETAILS_TP_ROUTIN.documentation:
Value: 11
Flag used with llGetParcelDetails to retrieve the teleport routing setting for this parcel (0 = TP_ROUTING_BLOCKED, 1 = TP_ROUTING_LANDINGP, 2 = TP_ROUTING_FREE; rules are only enforced if a landing point is set).

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_FLAG_ALLOW_ALL_OB.documentation:
Value: 0x08000000
Flag used with llGetParcelFlags to check if the parcel allows all objects to enter.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_FLAG_ALLOW_CREATE.documentation:
Value: 0x4000000
Flag used with llGetParcelFlags to check if group members or scripts in group-deeded objects are allowed to create/rez objects on the parcel.

Value: 0x40
Flag used with llGetParcelFlags to check if the parcel allows anyone to create objects.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_FLAG_ALLOW_DAMAGE.documentation:
Value: 0x20
Flag used with llGetParcelFlags to check if damage is enabled on the parcel.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_FLAG_ALLOW_FLY.documentation:
Value: 0x1
Flag used with llGetParcelFlags to check if flying is allowed on the parcel.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_FLAG_ALLOW_GROUP_.documentation:
Value: 0x10000000
Flag used with llGetParcelFlags to check if the parcel restricts object entry to only group-owned and owner-owned objects.

Value: 0x2000000
Flag used with llGetParcelFlags to check if the parcel allows scripts owned by the parcel's group to run.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_FLAG_ALLOW_LANDMA.documentation:
Value: 0x8
Flag used with llGetParcelFlags to check if the parcel allows landmarks to be created.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_FLAG_ALLOW_SCRIPT.documentation:
Value: 0x2
Flag used with llGetParcelFlags to check if scripts (including outside scripts) are allowed to run on the parcel.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_FLAG_ALLOW_TERRAF.documentation:
Value: 0x10
Flag used with llGetParcelFlags to check if the parcel allows anyone to terraform the land.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_FLAG_LOCAL_SOUND_.documentation:
Value: 0x8000
Flag used with llGetParcelFlags to check if the parcel restricts spatialized sounds to the parcel (preventing outside sound from being heard inside).

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_FLAG_RESTRICT_PUS.documentation:
Value: 0x200000
Flag used with llGetParcelFlags to check if the parcel restricts the use of llPushObject by non-owners (or non-group officers if group-owned).

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_FLAG_USE_ACCESS_G.documentation:
Value: 0x100
Flag used with llGetParcelFlags to check if the parcel limits access to group members.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_FLAG_USE_ACCESS_L.documentation:
Value: 0x200
Flag used with llGetParcelFlags to check if the parcel limits access to a specific list of residents (avatars).

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_FLAG_USE_BAN_LIST.documentation:
Value: 0x400
Flag used with llGetParcelFlags to check if the parcel uses a ban list (including restricting access based on payment information).

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_FLAG_USE_LAND_PAS.documentation:
Value: 0x800
Flag used with llGetParcelFlags to check if the parcel restricts access via purchasable land passes (sold for L$).

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_MEDIA_COMMAND_AGE.documentation:
Value: 7
Command used with llParcelMediaCommandList to apply the specified media command to the designated agent only.
