@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_MEDIA_COMMAND_AUT.documentation:
Value: 9
Command used with llParcelMediaCommandList to toggle or set the parcel media option 'Auto scale content'.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_MEDIA_COMMAND_DES.documentation:
Value: 12
Command used with llParcelMediaCommandList or llParcelMediaQuery to get or set the parcel media description.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_MEDIA_COMMAND_LOO.documentation:
Value: 3
Command used with llParcelMediaCommandList to start the media stream playing from the current frame and have it loop back to the beginning when it reaches the end.

Value: 13
Command used with llParcelMediaCommandList or llParcelMediaQuery to get or set the parcel's media looping variable or loop duration (which may have functionality limitations; see VWR-19712).

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_MEDIA_COMMAND_PAU.documentation:
Value: 1
Command used with llParcelMediaCommandList to pause the media stream on the current frame.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_MEDIA_COMMAND_PLA.documentation:
Value: 2
Command used with llParcelMediaCommandList to play the media stream from its current frame, stopping when the end is reached.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_MEDIA_COMMAND_SIZ.documentation:
Value: 11
Command used with llParcelMediaCommandList or llParcelMediaQuery to get or set the parcel media pixel resolution.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_MEDIA_COMMAND_STO.documentation:
Value: 0
Command used with llParcelMediaCommandList to stop the media stream and rewind it to the first frame.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_MEDIA_COMMAND_TEX.documentation:
Value: 4
Command used with llParcelMediaCommandList or llParcelMediaQuery to get or set the parcel's media texture.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_MEDIA_COMMAND_TIM.documentation:
Value: 6
Command used with llParcelMediaCommandList to jump to a specific timestamp in the media stream, specified in floating-point seconds.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_MEDIA_COMMAND_TYP.documentation:
Value: 10
Command used with llParcelMediaCommandList or llParcelMediaQuery to get or set the parcel media MIME type (e.g., 'text/html').

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_MEDIA_COMMAND_UNL.documentation:
Value: 8
Command used with llParcelMediaCommandList to completely unload the media and restore the face's original texture.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_MEDIA_COMMAND_URL.documentation:
Value: 5
Command used with llParcelMediaCommandList or llParcelMediaQuery to get or set the parcel's media URL.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_SALE_AGENT.documentation:
Value: 2
Option used with llSetParcelForSale to specify the agent ID authorized to buy the parcel. If none is set, any agent can purchase it.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_SALE_ERROR_BAD_PA.documentation:
Value: 5
Error code indicating the parameters provided to set the parcel sale information are invalid.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_SALE_ERROR_INVALI.documentation:
Value: 4
Error code indicating the specified parcel price is invalid (e.g., less than or equal to 0).

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_SALE_ERROR_IN_ESC.documentation:
Value: 3
Error code indicating the parcel is in escrow and cannot be put up for sale.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_SALE_ERROR_NO_PAR.documentation:
Value: 1
Error code indicating the parcel could not be found.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_SALE_ERROR_NO_PER.documentation:
Value: 2
Error code indicating the script lacks the required permissions to set the parcel sale information.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_SALE_OBJECTS.documentation:
Value: 3
Option used with llSetParcelForSale. If set to TRUE, objects on the parcel are included in the sale.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_SALE_OK.documentation:
Value: 0
Status code indicating the parcel sale information was successfully set.

@sl-slua/global/PARCEL_SALE_PRICE.documentation:
Value: 1
Option used with llSetParcelForSale to set the parcel price. If no authorized agent is set, the price must be greater than 0.

@sl-slua/global/PASSIVE.documentation:
Value: 0x4
Identifies static or non-moving in-world objects (which do not consume active server resources). In llDetectedType(), indicates non-physical objects. In llSensor()/llSensorRepeat() filters, searches for non-physical, non-scripted, inactive, or non-moving physical objects.

@sl-slua/global/PASS_ALWAYS.documentation:
Value: 1
Used with event-passing functions (e.g., llPassTouches, llPassCollisions) to ensure events are always passed from the child prim to the root prim, regardless of whether they are handled by child script handlers.

@sl-slua/global/PASS_IF_NOT_HANDLED.documentation:
Value: 0
Used with event-passing functions (default behavior) to pass events from the child prim to the root prim only if there is no script handling the event in the child.

@sl-slua/global/PASS_NEVER.documentation:
Value: 2
Used with event-passing functions to ensure events are never passed from the child prim to the root prim, regardless of whether they are handled by child script handlers.

@sl-slua/global/PATROL_PAUSE_AT_WAYPOINT.documentation:
Value: 0
Option parameter for llPatrolPoints (defaults to FALSE). If TRUE, the character slows down and momentarily pauses at each waypoint. If FALSE, the character moves to the next waypoint at full speed with no pause.

@sl-slua/global/PAYMENT_INFO_ON_FILE.documentation:
Value: 1
Agent data flag returned by llRequestAgentData indicating if the user has payment information on file.

@sl-slua/global/PAYMENT_INFO_USED.documentation:
Value: 2
Agent data flag returned by llRequestAgentData indicating if the user has ever used their payment information.

@sl-slua/global/PAY_DEFAULT.documentation:
Value: -2
Used with llSetPayPrice to use the default value for the specified quick pay button.

@sl-slua/global/PAY_HIDE.documentation:
Value: -1
Used with llSetPayPrice to hide the specified quick pay button completely.

@sl-slua/global/PERMISSION_ATTACH.documentation:
Value: 0x00020
Runtime permission that allows the script to successfully attach to or detach from the agent using llAttachToAvatar.

@sl-slua/global/PERMISSION_CHANGE_LINKS.documentation:
Value: 0x00080
Runtime permission that allows the script to successfully create, break, or modify links to other objects using llCreateLink, llBreakLink, or llBreakAllLinks.

@sl-slua/global/PERMISSION_CONTROL_CAMER.documentation:
Value: 0x00800
Runtime permission that allows the script to control the agent's camera. The agent must be sitting on or attached to the object, and permission is automatically revoked on standing up or detaching.

@sl-slua/global/PERMISSION_DEBIT.documentation:
Value: 0x00002
Runtime permission that allows the script to successfully call llGiveMoney or llTransferLindenDollars to debit the owner's account.

@sl-slua/global/PERMISSION_OVERRIDE_ANIM.documentation:
Value: 0x08000
Runtime permission that allows the script to configure and override default animations on the agent.

@sl-slua/global/PERMISSION_PRIVILEGED_LA.documentation:
Value: 0x80000
Runtime permission that grants the script privileged access to land parcel functions, which is required to use llSetParcelForSale.

@sl-slua/global/PERMISSION_RETURN_OBJECT.documentation:
Value: 0x10000
Runtime permission required to use the llReturnObjectsByID and llReturnObjectsByOwner functions to return objects from parcels.

@sl-slua/global/PERMISSION_SILENT_ESTATE.documentation:
Value: 0x04000
Runtime permission that allows the script to manage estate access rules via llManageEstateAccess without notifying the object owner of the changes.

@sl-slua/global/PERMISSION_TAKE_CONTROLS.documentation:
Value: 0x00004
Runtime permission that allows the script to successfully call the llTakeControls function to intercept the agent's controls.

@sl-slua/global/PERMISSION_TELEPORT.documentation:
Value: 0x01000
Runtime permission required to teleport the agent using the llTeleportAgent function.

@sl-slua/global/PERMISSION_TRACK_CAMERA.documentation:
Value: 0x00400
Runtime permission that allows the script to track the agent's camera position and rotation.

@sl-slua/global/PERMISSION_TRIGGER_ANIMA.documentation:
Value: 0x00010
Runtime permission that allows the script to start or stop animations on the agent (such as using llStartAnimation).

@sl-slua/global/PERM_ALL.documentation:
Value: 0x7FFFFFFF
Permissions mask representing a combination of move, modify, copy, and transfer permissions.

@sl-slua/global/PI.documentation:
Value: 3.14159265
The mathematical constant Pi (3.14159265), representing the number of radians in a half circle (semi-circle). When used in sensor functions, it specifies a full sphere scan.

@sl-slua/global/PING_PONG.documentation:
Value: 0x8
Used with texture animation functions to cause the animation to play forward first, and then in reverse.

@sl-slua/global/PI_BY_TWO.documentation:
Value: 1.57079633
The constant Pi/2 (1.57079633), representing the number of radians in a quarter circle. When used in sensor functions, it specifies a hemisphere scan.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_ALLOW_UNSIT.documentation:
Value: 39
Prim parameter for allowing or restricting manual standing for avatars seated on this prim within a valid experience. Ignored if the avatar was not seated via llSitOnLink. When restriction is active, participating seated avatars cannot manually stand or select another prim to sit on.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_ALPHA_MODE.documentation:
Value: 38
Prim parameter used to set diffuse texture alpha rendering mode attributes. Requires face, alpha_mode (PRIM_ALPHA_MODE_BLEND, _NONE, _MASK, or _EMISSIVE), and alpha_cutoff (used only for _MASK) parameters.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_ALPHA_MODE_BLEND.documentation:
Value: 1
Prim parameter setting for PRIM_ALPHA_MODE. Directs the face to use alpha blending for diffuse texture rendering (if an alpha channel exists). Also used as the default value to clear material settings from a prim face.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_ALPHA_MODE_EMISSIVE.documentation:
Value: 3
Prim parameter setting for PRIM_ALPHA_MODE. Renders the diffuse texture's alpha channel as an emissivity mask. Pixels render with an emissivity corresponding to their opacity under all lighting conditions (fully opaque pixels effectively render as 'full bright').

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_ALPHA_MODE_MASK.documentation:
Value: 2
Prim parameter setting for PRIM_ALPHA_MODE. Renders the diffuse texture's alpha channel on a binary per-pixel basis; pixels more opaque than the alpha cutoff render as fully opaque, while pixels below the cutoff render as fully transparent.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_ALPHA_MODE_NONE.documentation:
Value: 0
Prim parameter setting for PRIM_ALPHA_MODE. Directs the face to ignore the alpha channel of the diffuse texture and render as completely opaque (default setting when a material face lacks an alpha channel).

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_BUMP_BLOBS.documentation:
Value: 12
Face bump mapping setting: petridish (blobby amoeba-like shapes).

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_BUMP_BRIGHT.documentation:
Value: 1
Face bump mapping setting: brightness (generated from highlights).

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_BUMP_DARK.documentation:
Value: 2
Face bump mapping setting: darkness (generated from lowlights).

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_BUMP_DISKS.documentation:
Value: 10
Face bump mapping setting: discs (packed circles).

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_BUMP_LARGETILE.documentation:
Value: 14
Face bump mapping setting: stonetile (large tile).

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_BUMP_NONE.documentation:
Value: 0
Face bump mapping setting: none (no bump map).

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_BUMP_SHINY.documentation:
Value: 19
Prim parameter used to get or set both the shininess and bump mapping settings of a face.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_CAST_SHADOWS.documentation:
Value: 24
Deprecated prim parameter representing shadow casting settings for the primitive.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_CLICK_ACTION.documentation:
Value: 43
Prim parameter followed by a CLICK_ACTION_* constant to specify the default action taken when a user clicks or touches the prim.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_COLLISION_SOUND.documentation:
Value: 53
Prim parameter to set the collision sound UUID and volume level for the prim.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_COLOR.documentation:
Value: 18
Prim parameter to get or set the Blinn-Phong color and opacity of a face.

integer face – face number or ALL_SIDES
vector color – color in RGB (<0.0, 0.0, 0.0> = black, <1.0, 1.0, 1.0> = white)
float alpha – from 0.0 (clear) to 1.0 (solid) (0.0 <= alpha <= 1.0)

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_DAMAGE.documentation:
Value: 51
Prim parameter used to get or set the damage amount and damage type delivered by the prim on collision.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_DESC.documentation:
Value: 28
Prim parameter [PRIM_DESC, string description] to get or set the prim's description (equivalent to llGetObjectDesc/llSetObjectDesc).

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_FLEXIBLE.documentation:
Value: 21
Prim parameter to get or set the prim's flexible configuration.

integer boolean – TRUE enables, FALSE disables
integer softness – ranges from 0 to 3
float gravity – ranges from -10.0 to 10.0
float friction – ranges from 0.0 to 10.0
float wind – ranges from 0.0 to 10.0
float tension – ranges from 0.0 to 10.0
vector force

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_FULLBRIGHT.documentation:
Value: 20
Prim parameter [PRIM_FULLBRIGHT, integer face, integer boolean] to get or set whether full-bright rendering is enabled on the specified face.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_GLOW.documentation:
Value: 25
Prim parameter [PRIM_GLOW, integer face, float intensity] to get or set the glow intensity on a face (the compiler integer value is 25 if the named constant is rejected).

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_GLTF_ALPHA_MODE_BLE.documentation:
Value: 1
GLTF alpha mode setting for PRIM_GLTF_BASE_COLOR. Renders the material with transparency (alpha blending) in linear color space. Transparency from the texture is multiplied by the material's opacity multiplier. Note: This mode can suffer from depth sorting and performance issues.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_GLTF_ALPHA_MODE_MAS.documentation:
Value: 2
GLTF alpha mode setting for PRIM_GLTF_BASE_COLOR. Renders the material as fully opaque where the alpha value is greater than the alpha cutoff, and fully transparent otherwise.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_GLTF_ALPHA_MODE_OPA.documentation:
Value: 0
GLTF alpha mode setting for PRIM_GLTF_BASE_COLOR. Ignores the alpha channel entirely and renders the face material as completely opaque.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_GLTF_BASE_COLOR.documentation:
Value: 48
Prim parameter to get or set the GLTF base color override of an object's face. Requires face, texture, repeats, offsets, rotation, linear_color (in linear space; use llsRGB2Linear), alpha_mode (PRIM_GLTF_ALPHA_MODE_*), alpha_cutoff, and double_sided arguments.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_GLTF_EMISSIVE.documentation:
Value: 46
Prim parameter to get or set the GLTF emission override of a face. Requires face, texture, repeats, offsets, rotation, and emissive_tint (specified in linear space; use llsRGB2Linear).

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_GLTF_METALLIC_ROUGH.documentation:
Value: 47
Prim parameter to get or set the GLTF occlusion/metallic/roughness override of a face. Requires face, texture, repeats, offsets, rotation, metallic_factor, and roughness_factor arguments.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_GLTF_NORMAL.documentation:
Value: 45
Prim parameter to get or set the GLTF normal map override of a face. Requires face, texture, repeats, offsets, and rotation arguments.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_HEALTH.documentation:
Value: 52
Prim parameter used to get or set the health of the prim. Objects default to 0 health.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_HOLE_CIRCLE.documentation:
Value: 0x10
Parameter used with certain PRIM_TYPE_* flags to make a circular hole via the hole_shape parameter.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_HOLE_DEFAULT.documentation:
Value: 0x00
Parameter used with certain PRIM_TYPE_* flags to make a hole of the same shape as the outer shape via the hole_shape parameter.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_HOLE_SQUARE.documentation:
Value: 0x20
Parameter used with certain PRIM_TYPE_* flags to make a squarish hole via the hole_shape parameter.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_HOLE_TRIANGLE.documentation:
Value: 0x30
Parameter used with certain PRIM_TYPE_* flags to make a triangular hole via the hole_shape parameter.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_LINK_TARGET.documentation:
Value: 34
Prim parameter [PRIM_LINK_TARGET, integer link_target] used to get or set properties for multiple linked prims within a single primitive parameters call.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_MATERIAL.documentation:
Value: 2
Prim parameter [PRIM_MATERIAL, integer PRIM_MATERIAL_*] to get or set the prim's material. The material determines the default collision sound, sprite, friction coefficient, and restitution coefficient (elasticity) without affecting mass.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_MATERIAL_GLASS.documentation:
Value: 2
Prim material constant: glass (features very low friction).

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_MATERIAL_LIGHT.documentation:
Value: 7
Deprecated prim material constant. Light is now a face property instead of a prim property; equivalent functionality is achieved using [PRIM_FULLBRIGHT, ALL_SIDES, TRUE].

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_MEDIA_ALT_IMAGE_ENA.documentation:
Value: 0
Boolean. Gets or sets the default image state (the image a user sees before media is active) on a face. The default is specified by the server for that media type. Note: This flag is not currently implemented.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_MEDIA_AUTO_LOOP.documentation:
Value: 4
Boolean. Gets or sets whether auto-looping is enabled for the face media.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_MEDIA_AUTO_PLAY.documentation:
Value: 5
Boolean. Gets or sets whether media auto-plays when a resident is able to view it.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_MEDIA_AUTO_SCALE.documentation:
Value: 6
Boolean. Gets or sets whether auto-scaling is enabled, which forces the media to fill the entire texture size.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_MEDIA_AUTO_ZOOM.documentation:
Value: 7
Boolean. Gets or sets whether clicking the media triggers auto-zoom and auto-focus.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_MEDIA_CONTROLS.documentation:
Value: 1
Integer. Gets or sets the style of media controls (can be set to PRIM_MEDIA_CONTROLS_STANDARD or PRIM_MEDIA_CONTROLS_MINI).

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_MEDIA_CONTROLS_MINI.documentation:
Value: 1
Mini web navigation controls constant; does not include an address bar.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_MEDIA_CURRENT_URL.documentation:
Value: 2
String. Gets or sets the current URL displayed on the chosen face (maximum 1024 characters). Changing this URL triggers media navigation.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_MEDIA_FIRST_CLICK_I.documentation:
Value: 8
Boolean. Gets or sets what happens when someone click on a playing, but unfocused media face: TRUE: Does the same thing a focused media face does, and forwards the click to the web browser. FALSE: Focuses the media prim so that future clicks are forwarded to the web browser. Note: Viewer settings affect this flag. It works by default on most prims.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_MEDIA_HEIGHT_PIXELS.documentation:
Value: 10
Integer. Gets or sets the height of the media in pixels.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_MEDIA_HOME_URL.documentation:
Value: 3
String. Gets or sets the home URL for the chosen face (maximum 1024 characters).

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_MEDIA_PERMS_CONTROL.documentation:
Value: 14
Integer. Gets or sets the permissions mask controlling who can see the media control bar (PRIM_MEDIA_PERM_ANYONE, _GROUP, _NONE, or _OWNER).

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_MEDIA_PERMS_INTERAC.documentation:
Value: 13
Integer. Gets or sets the permissions mask controlling who can interact with the media (PRIM_MEDIA_PERM_ANYONE, _GROUP, _NONE, or _OWNER).

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_MEDIA_WHITELIST.documentation:
Value: 12
String. Gets or sets the whitelist as a string of escaped, comma-separated URLs (supports up to 64 URLs or 1024 characters, whichever comes first).

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_MEDIA_WHITELIST_ENA.documentation:
Value: 11
Boolean. Gets or sets whether media navigation is restricted to the URLs listed in PRIM_MEDIA_WHITELIST.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_MEDIA_WIDTH_PIXELS.documentation:
Value: 9
Integer. Gets or sets the width of the media in pixels.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_NAME.documentation:
Value: 27
Prim parameter [PRIM_NAME, string name] to get or set the prim's name (equivalent to llGetObjectName/llSetObjectName).

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_NORMAL.documentation:
Value: 37
Prim parameter to get or set the Blinn-Phong normal map texture settings of a face. Requires face, texture, repeats, offsets, and rotation_in_radians arguments.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_OMEGA.documentation:
Value: 32
Prim parameter to get, set, or disable a client-side physical-like rotation (analogous to llTargetOmega).

vector axis – arbitrary axis to rotate the object around
float spinrate – rate of rotation in radians per second
float gain – also modulates the final spinrate and disables the rotation behavior if zero

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_PHANTOM.documentation:
Value: 5
Prim parameter [PRIM_PHANTOM, integer boolean] to get or set whether phantom status is enabled.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_PHYSICS.documentation:
Value: 3
Prim parameter [PRIM_PHYSICS, integer boolean] to get or set whether the object responds to Second Life physics.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_PHYSICS_SHAPE_CONVE.documentation:
Value: 2
Physics shape type setting. Uses the convex hull of the prim shape to generate its physics representation (the default for mesh objects).

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_PHYSICS_SHAPE_NONE.documentation:
Value: 1
Physics shape type setting. Directs the physics engine to ignore this prim entirely, preventing it from contributing to the linkset's physics shape. Note: Cannot be applied to the root prim.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_PHYSICS_SHAPE_PRIM.documentation:
Value: 0
Physics shape type setting. Uses the actual visible shape of the prim to determine its physics representation (the default for all non-mesh objects).

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_PHYSICS_SHAPE_TYPE.documentation:
Value: 30
Prim parameter used to get or set the type of shape the physics engine uses for the prim (PRIM_PHYSICS_SHAPE_NONE, _PRIM, or _CONVEX). Mainly used for physics optimization.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_POINT_LIGHT.documentation:
Value: 23
Prim parameter to configure local lighting.
Accepts color in linear space (use llsRGB2Linear to convert from sRGB).

integer boolean – TRUE enables, FALSE disables
vector linear_color – linear color in RGB (<0.0, 0.0, 0.0> = black, <1.0, 1.0, 1.0> = white)
float intensity – ranges from 0.0 to 1.0
float radius – ranges from 0.1 to 20.0
float falloff – ranges from 0.01 to 2.0

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_POSITION.documentation:
Value: 6
Prim parameter [PRIM_POSITION, vector position] to get or set the position of the prim in region coordinates (or local coordinates depending on the script context).

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_POS_LOCAL.documentation:
Value: 33
Prim parameter [PRIM_POS_LOCAL, vector position] to get or set the local position of a prim (equivalent to llGetLocalPos/llSetPos).

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_PROJECTOR.documentation:
Value: 42
Prim parameter [PRIM_PROJECTOR, string texture, float fov, float focus, float ambiance] to configure light projection settings. If the prim is not configured as a projector, the texture key will return NULL_KEY.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_REFLECTION_PROBE.documentation:
Value: 44
Prim parameter to configure the object as a custom-placed reflection probe for Image-Based Lighting (IBL). Only affects objects inside its volume of influence.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_REFLECTION_PROBE_BO.documentation:
Value: 1
Flag option used with PRIM_REFLECTION_PROBE. When set, the reflection probe volume is a box; when unset, it defaults to a sphere.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_REFLECTION_PROBE_DY.documentation:
Value: 2
Flag option used with PRIM_REFLECTION_PROBE. When set, the probe dynamically includes avatars in its image-based lighting reflections (which carries a rendering performance cost).

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_REFLECTION_PROBE_MI.documentation:
Value: 4
Flag option used with PRIM_REFLECTION_PROBE. When set, intersecting low-roughness PBR materials act as mirrors. Note: Mirrors do not reflect avatars unless PRIM_REFLECTION_PROBE_DYNAMIC is also enabled, and they carry a performance cost.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_RENDER_MATERIAL.documentation:
Value: 49
Prim parameter [PRIM_RENDER_MATERIAL, integer face, string material] to get or set face material settings. Note: Setting this clears most PRIM_GLTF_* properties on the face, except for repeats, offsets, and rotation_in_radians.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_ROTATION.documentation:
Value: 8
Prim parameter [PRIM_ROTATION, rotation global_rot] to get or set the global rotation of the prim (equivalent to llGetRot/llSetRot; note that setting this on child prims may be broken).

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_ROT_LOCAL.documentation:
Value: 29
Prim parameter [PRIM_ROT_LOCAL, rotation local_rot] to get or set the local rotation of a prim (equivalent to llGetLocalRot/llSetLocalRot).

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_SCRIPTED_SIT_ONLY.documentation:
Value: 40
Prim parameter restricting manual sitting on this prim. When active, sitting can only be initiated via llSitOnLink; manual sitting attempts fail. This restriction applies even outside experience-enabled regions.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_SCULPT_FLAG_ANIMESH.documentation:
Value: 32
Read-only flag for PRIM_TYPE_SCULPT to query whether the object is an Animated Mesh (Animesh).

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_SCULPT_FLAG_INVERT.documentation:
Value: 64
Flag for PRIM_TYPE_SCULPT to render the sculpted prim inside-out by inverting the normals of each polygon.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_SCULPT_FLAG_MIRROR.documentation:
Value: 128
Flag for PRIM_TYPE_SCULPT to render a mirror-image of the sculpted prim, mirrored across the X-axis.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_SCULPT_TYPE_CYLINDE.documentation:
Value: 4
Sculpt type option for PRIM_TYPE_SCULPT that stitches the left side to the right to produce a cylinder-shaped sculpted prim.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_SCULPT_TYPE_MASK.documentation:
Value: 7
Bitmask used when parsing sculpted prim properties (PRIM_TYPE_SCULPT) from llGetPrimitiveParams to separate the base sculpt type from modifying flags (invert or mirror).

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_SCULPT_TYPE_MESH.documentation:
Value: 5
Sculpt type option for PRIM_TYPE_SCULPT indicating the object is a uploaded Mesh model.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_SCULPT_TYPE_PLANE.documentation:
Value: 3
Sculpt type option for PRIM_TYPE_SCULPT that performs no stitching or converging, producing a flat plane-shaped sculpted prim.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_SCULPT_TYPE_SPHERE.documentation:
Value: 1
Sculpt type option for PRIM_TYPE_SCULPT that stitches the left side to the right and separately converges the top and bottom to produce a sphere-shaped sculpted prim.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_SCULPT_TYPE_TORUS.documentation:
Value: 2
Sculpt type option for PRIM_TYPE_SCULPT that stitches top-to-bottom and left-to-right to produce a torus-shaped sculpted prim.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_SHINY_HIGH.documentation:
Value: 3
Sets the highest intensity legacy face shininess setting.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_SHINY_LOW.documentation:
Value: 1
Sets the lowest intensity legacy face shininess setting.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_SHINY_MEDIUM.documentation:
Value: 2
Sets a medium intensity legacy face shininess setting.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_SHINY_NONE.documentation:
Value: 0
Disables the legacy face shininess setting.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_SIT_FLAGS.documentation:
Value: 50
Prim parameter used to get or set sit-related flags currently active on the prim.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_SIT_TARGET.documentation:
Value: 41
Prim parameter [PRIM_SIT_TARGET, integer boolean, vector offset, rotation rot] to get or set the prim's sit target. Setting the boolean to FALSE deactivates the target. Unlike llLinkSitTarget, offset <0.0, 0.0, 0.0> can be explicitly set.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_SIZE.documentation:
Value: 7
Prim parameter [PRIM_SIZE, vector size] to get or set the prim's physical dimensions (equivalent to llGetScale/llSetScale).

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_SLICE.documentation:
Value: 35
Prim parameter [PRIM_SLICE, vector slice] to get or set the prim's slice values (a shape attribute equivalent to advanced cut).

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_SPECULAR.documentation:
Value: 36
Prim parameter to get or set the Blinn-Phong specular map texture settings of a face. Requires face, texture, repeats, offsets, rotation_in_radians, color, glossy, and environment arguments.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_TEMP_ON_REZ.documentation:
Value: 4
Prim parameter used to get or set the object's temporary status. Temporary objects live until the next garbage collection cycle (about 1 minute), do not count against normal prim limits, but are subject to regional limits.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_TEXGEN.documentation:
Value: 22
Prim parameter [PRIM_TEXGEN, integer face, integer mode] to get or set the texture mapping mode of the face.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_TEXGEN_DEFAULT.documentation:
Value: 0
Texture mapping mode setting for PRIM_TEXGEN. Specifies that texture repeat units are measured in texture repeats per face.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_TEXGEN_PLANAR.documentation:
Value: 1
Texture mapping mode setting for PRIM_TEXGEN. Specifies that texture repeat units are measured in repeats per half-meter (unlike the in-world editor, which measures in repeats per meter).

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_TEXT.documentation:
Value: 26
Prim parameter [PRIM_TEXT, string text, vector color, float alpha] to get or set the object's floating/hover text (equivalent to llSetText).

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_TEXTURE.documentation:
Value: 17
Prim parameter [PRIM_TEXTURE, integer face, string texture, vector repeats, vector offsets, float rotation_in_radians] to get or set the Blinn-Phong diffuse texture settings of a face.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_TYPE.documentation:
Value: 9
Prim parameter used to get or set the geometric shape type (PRIM_TYPE_*) and its associated parameters.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_TYPE_BOX.documentation:
Value: 0
Shape parameter for PRIM_TYPE used to define the prim as a box and configure its shape properties.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_TYPE_CYLINDER.documentation:
Value: 1
Shape parameter for PRIM_TYPE used to define the prim as a cylinder and configure its shape properties.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_TYPE_PRISM.documentation:
Value: 2
Shape parameter for PRIM_TYPE used to define the prim as a prism and configure its shape properties.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_TYPE_RING.documentation:
Value: 6
Shape parameter for PRIM_TYPE used to define the prim as a ring and configure its shape properties.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_TYPE_SCULPT.documentation:
Value: 7
Shape parameter for PRIM_TYPE used to define the prim as a sculpted prim (sculpty) or mesh of a specific type.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_TYPE_SPHERE.documentation:
Value: 3
Shape parameter for PRIM_TYPE used to define the prim as a sphere and configure its shape properties.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_TYPE_TORUS.documentation:
Value: 4
Shape parameter for PRIM_TYPE used to define the prim as a torus and configure its shape properties.

@sl-slua/global/PRIM_TYPE_TUBE.documentation:
Value: 5
Shape parameter for PRIM_TYPE used to define the prim as a tube and configure its shape properties.

@sl-slua/global/PROFILE_SCRIPT_MEMORY.documentation:
Value: 1
Enables script memory profiling, tracking the maximum amount of memory consumed while it is active.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_PART_BF_DEST_COLOR.documentation:
Value: 2
Scales the RGBA values by the RGBA values of the destination.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_PART_BF_ONE_MINUS_D.documentation:
Value: 4
Scales the RGBA values by the inverted RGBA values of the destination.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_PART_BF_ONE_MINUS_S.documentation:
Value: 9
Scales the RGBA values by the inverted alpha values of the particle source.

Value: 5
Scales the RGBA values by the inverted RGBA values of the particle source.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_PART_BF_SOURCE_ALPH.documentation:
Value: 7
Scales the RGBA values by the alpha values of the particle source.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_PART_BF_SOURCE_COLO.documentation:
Value: 3
Scales the RGBA values by the RGBA values of the particle source.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_PART_BF_ZERO.documentation:
Value: 1
Zeros out the source or destination RGBA values.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_PART_BLEND_FUNC_DES.documentation:
Value: 25
Integer parameter defining the destination blending function.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_PART_BLEND_FUNC_SOU.documentation:
Value: 24
Integer parameter defining the source blending function.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_PART_BOUNCE_MASK.documentation:
Value: 0x4
Particle flag causing particles to bounce off a plane at the emitter object's Z height.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_PART_EMISSIVE_MASK.documentation:
Value: 0x100
Particle flag that makes particles full-bright and unaffected by global lighting (sunlight). Otherwise, particles are lit by current global lighting or local point lights.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_PART_END_ALPHA.documentation:
Value: 4
Float parameter that determines the ending alpha (transparency) of the particles.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_PART_END_COLOR.documentation:
Value: 3
Vector parameter that determines the ending color of the particles.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_PART_END_GLOW.documentation:
Value: 27
Float parameter that determines the ending glow value of the particles.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_PART_END_SCALE.documentation:
Value: 6
Vector parameter defining the ending size of the particle billboard in meters (z-axis value is ignored).

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_PART_FLAGS.documentation:
Value: 0
Integer bitfield parameter specifying the simulation flags for emitted particles. Multiple flags can be combined using bitwise OR (|).

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_PART_FOLLOW_SRC_MAS.documentation:
Value: 0x10
Particle flag causing particle positions to remain relative to the position and movement of the emitter. Enabling this flag disables the PSYS_SRC_BURST_RADIUS rule.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_PART_FOLLOW_VELOCIT.documentation:
Value: 0x20
Particle flag causing particles to rotate and orient their vertical 'top' toward their direction of movement or emission. Otherwise, they remain vertically oriented with the top of the texture facing up.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_PART_INTERP_COLOR_M.documentation:
Value: 0x1
Particle flag causing the particle's color and alpha to smoothly interpolate from their START values to their END values over its lifetime.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_PART_INTERP_SCALE_M.documentation:
Value: 0x2
Particle flag causing the particle's size/scale to transition from its START setting to its END setting over its lifetime.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_PART_MAX_AGE.documentation:
Value: 7
Float parameter specifying the lifetime (age in seconds) of an individual particle before it dies.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_PART_RIBBON_MASK.documentation:
Value: 0x400
Particle flag that joins emitted particles into a continuous ribbon strip. Textures stretch to join adjacent edges. Width is controlled by the 'x' start/end scale; 'y' controls maximum visibility distance. Ribbon segments are not camera-facing, mimic the emitter's Z-axis, and require horizontal movement to render.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_PART_START_ALPHA.documentation:
Value: 2
Float parameter that determines the starting alpha (transparency) of the particles.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_PART_START_COLOR.documentation:
Value: 1
Vector parameter that determines the starting color of the particles.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_PART_START_GLOW.documentation:
Value: 26
Float parameter that determines the starting glow value of the particles.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_PART_START_SCALE.documentation:
Value: 5
Vector parameter defining the starting size of the particle billboard in meters (z-axis value is ignored).

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_PART_TARGET_LINEAR_.documentation:
Value: 0x80
Particle flag causing emitted particles to move in a straight, evenly-spaced line toward the PSYS_SRC_TARGET_KEY target. Ignores non-DROP patterns, radius, burst speeds, angles, omega, acceleration, and wind. Combining with bounce while target is below emitter deflects particles upward.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_PART_TARGET_POS_MAS.documentation:
Value: 0x40
Particle flag causing emitted particles to change course and travel toward the target specified by PSYS_SRC_TARGET_KEY during their lifetime. If no valid or reachable target is set, particles target the emitting prim itself.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_PART_WIND_MASK.documentation:
Value: 0x8
Particle flag that applies wind as a secondary force on the particles, damping their velocity toward the wind velocity.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_SRC_ACCEL.documentation:
Value: 8
Vector parameter specifying the constant acceleration to apply to particles.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_SRC_ANGLE_BEGIN.documentation:
Value: 22
Float parameter specifying the angle in radians where particles will not be created (for ANGLE patterns).

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_SRC_ANGLE_END.documentation:
Value: 23
Float parameter specifying the ending angle in radians to be filled with particles (for ANGLE patterns). If smaller than angle_begin, their roles are swapped.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_SRC_BURST_PART_COUN.documentation:
Value: 15
Integer parameter specifying the number of particles to release in each burst.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_SRC_BURST_RADIUS.documentation:
Value: 16
Float parameter specifying the radial distance from the emitter's center at which particles are created.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_SRC_BURST_RATE.documentation:
Value: 13
Float parameter specifying the interval in seconds between particle bursts.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_SRC_BURST_SPEED_MAX.documentation:
Value: 18
Float parameter specifying the maximum initial speed of emitted particles.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_SRC_BURST_SPEED_MIN.documentation:
Value: 17
Float parameter specifying the minimum initial speed of emitted particles.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_SRC_INNERANGLE.documentation:
Value: 10
Float parameter specifying the inner angle of the arc for ANGLE or ANGLE_CONE patterns. The specified inner area will not contain particles.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_SRC_MAX_AGE.documentation:
Value: 19
Float parameter specifying the active duration of the particle system in seconds (0.0 means forever).

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_SRC_OMEGA.documentation:
Value: 21
Vector parameter setting the angular velocity used to rotate the emission axis for ANGLE and ANGLE_CONE patterns.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_SRC_OUTERANGLE.documentation:
Value: 11
Float parameter specifying the outer angle of the arc for ANGLE or ANGLE_CONE patterns. The area between the outer and inner angles is filled with particles.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_SRC_PATTERN.documentation:
Value: 9
Integer parameter specifying the generation pattern (PSYS_SRC_PATTERN_*) used to emit particles.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_SRC_PATTERN_ANGLE.documentation:
Value: 0x04
Emission pattern that sprays particles outward in a flat circular, semi-circular, arc, or ray-shaped 2D area (around the prim's local X-axis) as defined by the start/end and inner/outer angle parameters.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_SRC_PATTERN_ANGLE_C.documentation:
Value: 0x08
Emission pattern that sprays particles outward in a 3D spherical, conical, or ring-shaped area defined by the start/end and inner/outer angle parameters. Can emulate the EXPLODE pattern if angles are set to 0.0 and PI.

Value: 0x10
Incomplete emission pattern that behaves identically to the DROP pattern. Intended to invert the ANGLE parameters to define an empty area where particles are not sprayed.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_SRC_PATTERN_DROP.documentation:
Value: 0x01
Emission pattern that drops particles at the source position with no initial velocity. Overrides burst radius, minimum speed, and maximum speed settings to 0.0.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_SRC_PATTERN_EXPLODE.documentation:
Value: 0x02
Emission pattern that shoots particles outward in all directions using the burst parameters.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_SRC_TARGET_KEY.documentation:
Value: 20
Key (UUID) of the target object that particles travel toward when PSYS_PART_TARGET_POS_MASK or PSYS_PART_TARGET_LINEAR_MASK is enabled.

@sl-slua/global/PSYS_SRC_TEXTURE.documentation:
Value: 12
String parameter specifying the inventory name or UUID asset key of the texture used for the particles.

@sl-slua/global/PUBLIC_CHANNEL.documentation:
Value: 0
Integer constant representing the open local chat channel. Passing this to chat functions (llSay, llWhisper, llShout) prints text to the publicly heard chat seen by nearby users and objects.

@sl-slua/global/PURSUIT_FUZZ_FACTOR.documentation:
Value: 3
Option for llPursue that selects a random destination near the PURSUIT_OFFSET (range is 0 to 1, where 1 is most random). Requires a non-zero PURSUIT_OFFSET.

@sl-slua/global/PURSUIT_GOAL_TOLERANCE.documentation:
Value: 5
Option for llPursue defining how close (between 0.25m and 10m) the character must be to consider the goal reached.

@sl-slua/global/PURSUIT_INTERCEPT.documentation:
Value: 4
Option for llPursue specifying whether the pathfinding character attempts to predict and intercept the target's future location.

@sl-slua/global/PURSUIT_OFFSET.documentation:
Value: 1
Option for llPursue specifying a constant position offset relative to the target for the character to navigate toward.

@sl-slua/global/PU_EVADE_HIDDEN.documentation:
Value: 0x07
Path update status code triggered when an llEvade character determines it has successfully hidden from its pursuer.

@sl-slua/global/PU_EVADE_SPOTTED.documentation:
Value: 0x08
Path update status code triggered when an llEvade character switches from hiding to running.

@sl-slua/global/PU_FAILURE_DYNAMIC_PATHF.documentation:
Value: 10
Path update failure code triggered when a character enters a region where dynamic pathfinding has been disabled in the Region Debug Console.

@sl-slua/global/PU_FAILURE_INVALID_GOAL.documentation:
Value: 0x03
Path update failure code triggered when the specified goal is not on the region's navigation mesh (navmesh) and is unreachable.

@sl-slua/global/PU_FAILURE_INVALID_START.documentation:
Value: 0x02
Path update failure code triggered when the character is at an unnavigable starting position (e.g., off the navmesh or too high above it).

@sl-slua/global/PU_FAILURE_NO_NAVMESH.documentation:
Value: 0x09
Path update fatal error triggered when there is no navmesh generated for the region (typically indicates a server failure).

@sl-slua/global/PU_FAILURE_NO_VALID_DEST.documentation:
Value: 0x06
Path update failure code triggered when no reachable destinations remain (e.g., all patrol waypoints are blocked).

@sl-slua/global/PU_FAILURE_OTHER.documentation:
Value: 1000000
Path update failure code representing an unspecified or general failure.

@sl-slua/global/PU_FAILURE_PARCEL_UNREAC.documentation:
Value: 11
Path update failure code triggered when a character is blocked from entering a parcel (e.g., the parcel is full or object entry was disabled after the navmesh was baked).

@sl-slua/global/PU_FAILURE_TARGET_GONE.documentation:
Value: 0x05
Path update failure code triggered when an llPursue or llEvade target can no longer be tracked (e.g., they left the region or went more than ~30m outside region boundaries).

@sl-slua/global/PU_FAILURE_UNREACHABLE.documentation:
Value: 0x04
Path update failure code triggered when a previously valid goal becomes unreachable (e.g., an obstacle blocks the path).

@sl-slua/global/PU_GOAL_REACHED.documentation:
Value: 0x01
Path update status code triggered when the character reaches its goal and either stops or chooses a new goal (if wandering).

@sl-slua/global/PU_SLOWDOWN_DISTANCE_REA.documentation:
Value: 0x00
Path update status code triggered when the character enters the slowdown distance near its current goal.

@sl-slua/global/RAD_TO_DEG.documentation:
Value: 57.2957795
The constant 57.2957795 (precise value is 180/PI). Multiply a value in radians by this number to convert it to degrees.

@sl-slua/global/RCERR_CAST_TIME_EXCEEDED.documentation:
Value: -3
Raycast error indicating the parcel or agent exceeded the maximum allocated raycast time. Waiting a few frames and retrying will usually succeed as resources replenish.

@sl-slua/global/RCERR_SIM_PERF_LOW.documentation:
Value: -2
Raycast error indicating the request failed due to low simulator performance. Wait before retrying, and if possible, reduce scene complexity.

@sl-slua/global/RCERR_UNKNOWN.documentation:
Value: -1
Raycast error indicating an unspecified failure.

@sl-slua/global/RC_DATA_FLAGS.documentation:
Value: 2
Flag used with llCastRay to configure returned data.

@sl-slua/global/RC_DETECT_PHANTOM.documentation:
Value: 1
Flag used with llCastRay. If set to TRUE (or non-zero), detects both phantom and volume-detect objects (they cannot be filtered individually). This overrides the RC_REJECT_NONPHYSICAL and RC_REJECT_PHYSICAL settings in RC_REJECT_TYPES.

@sl-slua/global/RC_GET_LINK_NUM.documentation:
Value: 4
Flag used with llCastRay causing the results stride to include the specific link number that was hit.

@sl-slua/global/RC_GET_NORMAL.documentation:
Value: 1
Flag used with llCastRay causing the results stride to include the surface normal vector of the impact point.

@sl-slua/global/RC_GET_ROOT_KEY.documentation:
Value: 2
Flag used with llCastRay that returns the key of the hit object's root prim instead of the hit child prim.

@sl-slua/global/RC_MAX_HITS.documentation:
Value: 3
Flag used with llCastRay to set the maximum number of hits to return (up to 256). To avoid performance issues, keep this value small.

@sl-slua/global/RC_REJECT_AGENTS.documentation:
Value: 1
Flag used with llCastRay to prevent the detection of avatars.

@sl-slua/global/RC_REJECT_LAND.documentation:
Value: 8
Flag used with llCastRay to ignore the actual terrain ground (land).

@sl-slua/global/RC_REJECT_NONPHYSICAL.documentation:
Value: 4
Flag used with llCastRay to ignore non-physical objects.

@sl-slua/global/RC_REJECT_PHYSICAL.documentation:
Value: 2
Flag used with llCastRay to ignore physical objects.

@sl-slua/global/RC_REJECT_TYPES.documentation:
Value: 0
Flag used with llCastRay to define a mask for ignoring specific types of objects and avatars.

@sl-slua/global/REGION_FLAG_ALLOW_DAMAGE.documentation:
Value: 0x1
Flag used with llGetRegionFlags to check if the region is entirely damage-enabled.

@sl-slua/global/REGION_FLAG_ALLOW_DIRECT.documentation:
Value: 0x100000
Flag used with llGetRegionFlags to check if direct teleportation is allowed in the region.

@sl-slua/global/REGION_FLAG_BLOCK_FLY.documentation:
Value: 0x80000
Flag used with llGetRegionFlags to check if flying is blocked/disabled in the region.

@sl-slua/global/REGION_FLAG_BLOCK_TERRAF.documentation:
Value: 0x40
Flag used with llGetRegionFlags to check if terraforming is disabled in the region.

@sl-slua/global/REGION_FLAG_DISABLE_COLL.documentation:
Value: 0x1000
Flag used with llGetRegionFlags to check if collisions have been disabled in the region.

@sl-slua/global/REGION_FLAG_DISABLE_PHYS.documentation:
Value: 0x4000
Flag used with llGetRegionFlags to check if physics has been disabled in the region.

@sl-slua/global/REGION_FLAG_FIXED_SUN.documentation:
Value: 0x10
Flag used with llGetRegionFlags to check if the sun's position is fixed in the region.

@sl-slua/global/REGION_FLAG_RESTRICT_PUS.documentation:
Value: 0x400000
Flag used with llGetRegionFlags to check if llPushObject is restricted in the region.

@sl-slua/global/REGION_FLAG_SANDBOX.documentation:
Value: 0x100
Flag used with llGetRegionFlags to check if the region is designated as a sandbox.

@sl-slua/global/REQUIRE_LINE_OF_SIGHT.documentation:
Value: 2
Option flag for llPursue defining whether the character requires a physical line-of-sight to chase the target. When active, it will not target positions blocked by solid obstacles.

@sl-slua/global/RESTITUTION.documentation:
Value: 4
Used with llSetPhysicsMaterial to enable the restitution (bounciness) value (must be between 0.0 and 1.0) which overrides the previous value.

@sl-slua/global/REVERSE.documentation:
Value: 0x4
Used with texture animation functions to cause the animation to play in reverse (from end to start).

@sl-slua/global/REZ_ACCEL.documentation:
Value: 5
Rez parameter specifying a constant acceleration force to apply to the rezzed object (as a [vector force, integer relative] pair). If relative is TRUE, the vector is in local coordinates.

@sl-slua/global/REZ_DAMAGE.documentation:
Value: 8
Rez parameter specifying the float damage amount the rezzed object inflicts on an agent upon collision.

@sl-slua/global/REZ_DAMAGE_TYPE.documentation:
Value: 12
Rez parameter specifying the integer damage type applied on collision (supports DAMAGE_TYPE_* constants, custom types, or a repurposed damage field).

@sl-slua/global/REZ_FLAGS.documentation:
Value: 1
Rez parameter specifying the integer bitfield of flags to apply to the newly created object.

@sl-slua/global/REZ_FLAG_BLOCK_GRAB_OBJE.documentation:
Value: 0x0080
Rez flag that disables grabbing on the newly rezzed object.

@sl-slua/global/REZ_FLAG_DIE_ON_COLLIDE.documentation:
Value: 0x0008
Rez flag causing the object to die/delete after its first collision.

@sl-slua/global/REZ_FLAG_DIE_ON_NOENTRY.documentation:
Value: 0x0010
Rez flag causing the object to die/delete if it attempts to enter an unauthorized parcel.

@sl-slua/global/REZ_FLAG_NO_COLLIDE_FAMI.documentation:
Value: 0x0040
Rez flag preventing collision events from triggering when colliding with other objects created by the same rezzer.

@sl-slua/global/REZ_FLAG_NO_COLLIDE_OWNE.documentation:
Value: 0x0020
Rez flag preventing collision events from triggering when colliding with its owner.

@sl-slua/global/REZ_FLAG_PHANTOM.documentation:
Value: 0x0004
Rez flag causing the object to be created as phantom.

@sl-slua/global/REZ_FLAG_PHYSICAL.documentation:
Value: 0x0002
Rez flag causing the object to be created with physics enabled.

@sl-slua/global/REZ_FLAG_TEMP.documentation:
Value: 0x0001
Rez flag causing the object to be created as temporary.

@sl-slua/global/REZ_LOCK_AXES.documentation:
Value: 11
Rez parameter [vector locks] used to prevent the physical object from rotating or spinning around specified axes. Setting a coordinate to a non-zero value locks that axis.

@sl-slua/global/REZ_OMEGA.documentation:
Value: 7
Rez parameter specifying the initial angular velocity (omega) applied to the object. Accepts [vector axis, integer relative, float spin, float gain]. If relative is TRUE, the axis vector is in local coordinates.

@sl-slua/global/REZ_PARAM.documentation:
Value: 0
Rez parameter specifying the integer start parameter passed into the newly rezzed object's on_rez event.

@sl-slua/global/REZ_PARAM_STRING.documentation:
Value: 13
Rez parameter specifying an initialization string (up to 1024 bytes) passed to the root prim of the newly rezzed object, readable via llGetStartString.

@sl-slua/global/REZ_POS.documentation:
Value: 2
Rez parameter [vector position, integer relative, integer at_root] to specify where to rez the object. Can be in region coordinates or relative to the rezzing object, and centered on either the object's center or the root prim.

@sl-slua/global/REZ_ROT.documentation:
Value: 3
Rez parameter specifying the initial rotation to apply to the newly rezzed object. Can be relative to the rezzing object or absolute.

@sl-slua/global/REZ_SOUND.documentation:
Value: 9
Rez parameter [string name_or_uuid, float volume, integer loop] to attach and play a sound from inventory or a UUID asset key. If loop is TRUE, it loops continuously.

@sl-slua/global/REZ_SOUND_COLLIDE.documentation:
Value: 10
Rez parameter [string name_or_uuid, float volume] specifying a sound (from inventory or a UUID asset key) to play upon collision with another object, the ground, or an avatar.

@sl-slua/global/REZ_VEL.documentation:
Value: 4
Rez parameter specifying the initial velocity to apply to the object. Accepts [vector velocity, integer local, integer inherit]. If inherit is TRUE, it adds the rezzing object's velocity.

@sl-slua/global/ROTATE.documentation:
Value: 0x20
Used with texture animation functions to animate the texture's rotation. Cannot be combined with SCALE.

@sl-slua/global/SCALE.documentation:
Value: 0x40
Used with texture animation functions to animate the texture's scale. Cannot be combined with ROTATE.

@sl-slua/global/SCRIPTED.documentation:
Value: 0x8
Identifies scripted in-world objects. In llDetectedType(), indicates the target has at least one active script. In llSensor()/llSensorRepeat() filters, searches for objects containing active scripts that are currently running.

@sl-slua/global/SIM_STAT_ACTIVE_SCRIPT_C.documentation:
Value: 12
Returns the total number of active scripts in the region.

@sl-slua/global/SIM_STAT_AGENT_COUNT.documentation:
Value: 10
Returns the total number of agents in the region.

@sl-slua/global/SIM_STAT_AGENT_MS.documentation:
Value: 7
Returns the time spent in the 'agent' segment of the simulation frame.

@sl-slua/global/SIM_STAT_AGENT_UPDATES.documentation:
Value: 2
Returns the number of agent updates per second.

@sl-slua/global/SIM_STAT_AI_MS.documentation:
Value: 26
Returns the time spent on the AI step.

@sl-slua/global/SIM_STAT_ASSET_DOWNLOADS.documentation:
Value: 15
Returns the pending asset download count.

@sl-slua/global/SIM_STAT_ASSET_UPLOADS.documentation:
Value: 16
Returns the pending asset upload count.

@sl-slua/global/SIM_STAT_CHILD_AGENT_COU.documentation:
Value: 11
Returns the number of child (neighboring) agents in the region.

@sl-slua/global/SIM_STAT_IMAGE_MS.documentation:
Value: 8
Returns the time spent in the 'image' segment of the simulation frame.

@sl-slua/global/SIM_STAT_NET_MS.documentation:
Value: 4
Returns the time spent in the 'network' segment of the simulation frame.

@sl-slua/global/SIM_STAT_OTHER_MS.documentation:
Value: 5
Returns the time spent in the main simulation 'other' segment of the frame, which encapsulates task, script, and miscellaneous updates.

@sl-slua/global/SIM_STAT_PACKETS_IN.documentation:
Value: 13
Returns the average number of incoming packets per second.

@sl-slua/global/SIM_STAT_PACKETS_OUT.documentation:
Value: 14
Returns the average number of outgoing packets per second.

@sl-slua/global/SIM_STAT_PCT_CHARS_STEPP.documentation:
Value: 0
Returns the percentage of pathfinding characters updated (or skipped) each frame, averaged over the last minute. Corresponds to the 'Characters Updated' stat in the viewer's Statistics Bar.

@sl-slua/global/SIM_STAT_PHYSICS_FPS.documentation:
Value: 1
Returns the physics simulation frames per second (FPS).

@sl-slua/global/SIM_STAT_PHYSICS_MS.documentation:
Value: 6
Returns the time spent in the 'physics' segment of the simulation frame.

@sl-slua/global/SIM_STAT_PHYSICS_OTHER_M.documentation:
Value: 20
Returns the average update time for the physics 'other' segment.

@sl-slua/global/SIM_STAT_PHYSICS_SHAPE_M.documentation:
Value: 19
Returns the average update time for the physics 'shape' segment.

@sl-slua/global/SIM_STAT_PHYSICS_STEP_MS.documentation:
Value: 18
Returns the average physics step time.

@sl-slua/global/SIM_STAT_SCRIPT_EPS.documentation:
Value: 21
Returns the number of script events per second.

@sl-slua/global/SIM_STAT_SCRIPT_MS.documentation:
Value: 9
Returns the time spent in the 'script' segment of the simulation frame.

@sl-slua/global/SIM_STAT_SCRIPT_RUN_PCT.documentation:
Value: 25
Returns the percentage of scripts run during the frame.

@sl-slua/global/SIM_STAT_SPARE_MS.documentation:
Value: 22
Returns the spare time left after the frame.

@sl-slua/global/SIM_STAT_UNACKED_BYTES.documentation:
Value: 17
Returns the total number of unacknowledged bytes.

@sl-slua/global/SIT_FLAG_ALLOW_UNSIT.documentation:
Value: 0x0002
Sit flag indicating that a seated avatar is allowed to manually stand up (unsit) from a sit target. Applies only to agents who were seated via an LSL script like llSitOnLink.

@sl-slua/global/SIT_FLAG_NO_COLLIDE.documentation:
Value: 0x0010
Sit flag that disables the avatar's collision volume/hitbox while seated on this sit target.

@sl-slua/global/SIT_FLAG_NO_DAMAGE.documentation:
Value: 0x0020
Sit flag that prevents damage from being distributed or forwarded to agents sitting on this sit target.

@sl-slua/global/SIT_FLAG_SCRIPTED_ONLY.documentation:
Value: 0x0004
Sit flag that restricts sits to script-controlled actions only, preventing avatars from manually sitting on this prim.

@sl-slua/global/SIT_FLAG_SIT_TARGET.documentation:
Value: 0x0001
Read-only sit flag indicating whether the prim has an active sit target. Set or cleared using llSitTarget, llLinkSitTarget, or PRIM_SIT_TARGET, and read via llGetLinkSitFlags or PRIM_SIT_FLAGS.

@sl-slua/global/SIT_INVALID_AGENT.documentation:
Value: -4
Sit error code indicating that the specified agent/avatar ID could not be found or is invalid.

@sl-slua/global/SIT_INVALID_LINK.documentation:
Value: -5
Sit error code indicating that the link ID does not specify a valid prim, is not found, or resolves to multiple prims.

@sl-slua/global/SIT_INVALID_OBJECT.documentation:
Value: -7
Sit error code returned when attempting to force an avatar to sit on an invalid target (such as an attachment) that cannot be sat upon.

@sl-slua/global/SIT_NOT_EXPERIENCE.documentation:
Value: -1
Sit error code returned if the script is not running within a valid experience, lacks a valid experience key, or the experience is not permitted at the current location.

@sl-slua/global/SIT_NO_ACCESS.documentation:
Value: -6
Sit error code indicating that the avatar lacks access to the parcel where the target prim/linkset is located.

@sl-slua/global/SIT_NO_EXPERIENCE_PERMIS.documentation:
Value: -2
Sit error code indicating that the agent has not granted experience permissions to force sits.

@sl-slua/global/SIT_NO_SIT_TARGET.documentation:
Value: -3
Sit error code indicating that no open or available sit target could be found in the linkset.

@sl-slua/global/SIT_OK.documentation:
Value: 1
Sit error code indicating that the avatar was seated successfully.

@sl-slua/global/SKY_AMBIENT.documentation:
Value: 0
Environmental setting for the ambient color used in the scene.

@sl-slua/global/SKY_BLUE.documentation:
Value: 22
Environmental setting containing the colors used to calculate blue density and blue horizon in the sky.

@sl-slua/global/SKY_CLOUDS.documentation:
Value: 2
Environmental cloud parameters including color, coverage percentage, scale, variance, scroll speed (X/Y), density, detail exponents, and default texture status.

@sl-slua/global/SKY_CLOUD_TEXTURE.documentation:
Value: 19
Environmental setting containing the inventory name or UUID of the texture used for the clouds.

@sl-slua/global/SKY_DOME.documentation:
Value: 4
Environmental setting containing sky dome parameters: offset, radius, and maximum altitude.

@sl-slua/global/SKY_GAMMA.documentation:
Value: 5
Environmental setting for the gamma value applied to the scene (repurposed in viewer 7.0+ as the HDR Scale value in the EEP editor).

@sl-slua/global/SKY_GLOW.documentation:
Value: 6
Environmental setting for sun and moon glow parameters: size and focus.

@sl-slua/global/SKY_HAZE.documentation:
Value: 23
Environmental setting containing the values used to calculate the light scattering impact of blue density and blue horizon on the scene.

@sl-slua/global/SKY_LIGHT.documentation:
Value: 8
Environmental setting containing lighting parameters: dominant light direction (unit vector), current light color (fade_color in sRGB), and total ambient color (sRGB).

@sl-slua/global/SKY_MOON.documentation:
Value: 9
Environmental moon parameters: rotation, scale, brightness, is_default_texture, direction vector, ambient color, and diffuse color.

@sl-slua/global/SKY_MOON_TEXTURE.documentation:
Value: 20
Environmental setting containing the inventory name or UUID of the texture used for the moon.

@sl-slua/global/SKY_PLANET.documentation:
Value: 10
Environmental planet rendering parameters: planet_radius, sky_bottom_radius, and sky_top_radius.

@sl-slua/global/SKY_REFLECTION_PROBE_AMB.documentation:
Value: 24
Environmental setting for the minimum ambiance value of all reflection probes. Note: Supported in the GLTF Materials project, requiring compatible viewers and testing areas.

@sl-slua/global/SKY_REFRACTION.documentation:
Value: 11
Environmental sky refraction parameters for rainbows and optical effects: moisture_level, droplet_radius, and ice_level.

@sl-slua/global/SKY_STAR_BRIGHTNESS.documentation:
Value: 13
Environmental setting for the brightness value applied to stars.

@sl-slua/global/SKY_SUN.documentation:
Value: 14
Environmental sun parameters: rotation, scale, color, is_default_texture, direction vector, ambient color, and diffuse color.

@sl-slua/global/SKY_SUN_TEXTURE.documentation:
Value: 21
Environmental setting containing the inventory name or UUID of the texture used for the sun.

@sl-slua/global/SKY_TEXTURE_DEFAULTS.documentation:
Value: 1
Environmental check returning 1 if the sky textures are set to their default values, or 0 if they use custom textures.

@sl-slua/global/SKY_TRACKS.documentation:
Value: 15
Environmental setting containing the track altitudes used for sky transitions in the region.

@sl-slua/global/SMOOTH.documentation:
Value: 0x10
Used with texture animation functions to cause the animation to slide smoothly in the X direction (or transition smoothly in SCALE/ROTATE modes) instead of making instant frame changes.

@sl-slua/global/SOUND_LOOP.documentation:
Value: 0x01
Sound playback option that loops the sound continuously on the prim until stopped.

@sl-slua/global/SOUND_PLAY.documentation:
Value: 0x00
Sound playback option (default) that plays the sound once, attached to the prim.

@sl-slua/global/SOUND_SYNC.documentation:
Value: 0x04
Sound playback option that synchronizes playback to the nearest active sound master (see llLoopSoundMaster).

@sl-slua/global/SOUND_TRIGGER.documentation:
Value: 0x02
Sound playback option that triggers a non-attached sound once at the prim's current location (does not move with the prim). This flag overrides all other sound playback options.

@sl-slua/global/SQRT2.documentation:
Value: 1.41421356
The mathematical constant representing the square root of 2 (1.41421356).

@sl-slua/global/STATUS_BLOCK_GRAB.documentation:
Value: 0x40
Status flag (default FALSE) that blocks click-and-drag grab movements on unlinked prims or the root prim of a linkset. Useful for preventing physical objects from being trivially disturbed.

@sl-slua/global/STATUS_BLOCK_GRAB_OBJECT.documentation:
Value: 0x400
Status flag that blocks click-and-drag grab movements on all prims across the entire linkset.

@sl-slua/global/STATUS_BOUNDS_ERROR.documentation:
Value: 1002
Status code indicating that one or more arguments passed to the function had a bounds error.

@sl-slua/global/STATUS_CAST_SHADOWS.documentation:
Value: 0x200
Unused legacy status flag intended to configure an object's ability to cast shadows.

@sl-slua/global/STATUS_DIE_AT_EDGE.documentation:
Value: 0x80
Status flag (default TRUE) that causes the object to be deleted (and not returned) if it goes off-world (useful for bullets or rockets). Overridden by STATUS_RETURN_AT_EDGE.

@sl-slua/global/STATUS_DIE_AT_NO_ENTRY.documentation:
Value: 0x800
Status flag (default FALSE) that causes the object to be deleted if it attempts to enter an unauthorized or full parcel. No-copy objects ignore this setting and remain in-world.

@sl-slua/global/STATUS_INTERNAL_ERROR.documentation:
Value: 1999
Status code indicating an internal error occurred.

@sl-slua/global/STATUS_MALFORMED_PARAMS.documentation:
Value: 1000
Status code indicating the function was called with malformed parameters.

@sl-slua/global/STATUS_NOT_FOUND.documentation:
Value: 1003
Status code indicating that the specified object or item was not found.

@sl-slua/global/STATUS_NOT_SUPPORTED.documentation:
Value: 1004
Status code indicating the requested feature is not supported.

@sl-slua/global/STATUS_OK.documentation:
Value: 0
Status code indicating the function call completed successfully.

@sl-slua/global/STATUS_PHANTOM.documentation:
Value: 0x10
Status flag (default FALSE) that makes the entire object phantom/non-colliding when set to TRUE, allowing avatars and objects to pass through it. It is a good idea to use this for most objects that move or rotate, but are non-physical. It is also useful for simulating volumetric lighting.

@sl-slua/global/STATUS_PHYSICS.documentation:
Value: 0x1
Status flag (default FALSE) that controls whether the object responds to physical interactions, gravity, and forces.

@sl-slua/global/STATUS_RETURN_AT_EDGE.documentation:
Value: 0x100
Status flag causing the object to be returned to its owner when it goes off-world, overriding STATUS_DIE_AT_EDGE.

@sl-slua/global/STATUS_ROTATE_X.documentation:
Value: 0x2
Status flag (default TRUE) that allows physical rotation on the object's local X-axis. Setting to FALSE prevents physical rotation around the local X-axis. For example, a sit-and-spin device spins around the Z axis (up) but not around the X or Y axes.

@sl-slua/global/STATUS_ROTATE_Y.documentation:
Value: 0x4
Status flag (default TRUE) that allows physical rotation on the object's local Y-axis. Setting to FALSE prevents physical rotation around the local X-axis. For example, a sit-and-spin device spins around the Z axis (up) but not around the X or Y axes.

@sl-slua/global/STATUS_ROTATE_Z.documentation:
Value: 0x8
Status flag (default TRUE) that allows physical rotation on the object's local Z-axis. Setting to FALSE prevents physical rotation around the local Z-axis.

@sl-slua/global/STATUS_SANDBOX.documentation:
Value: 0x20
Status flag (default FALSE) that restricts the object's physical movement to within its creation region and a short distance (10 to 20 meters) from its creation point to prevent it from escaping.

@sl-slua/global/STATUS_TYPE_MISMATCH.documentation:
Value: 1001
Status code indicating that one or more arguments passed to the function had a type mismatch.

@sl-slua/global/STATUS_WHITELIST_FAILED.documentation:
Value: 2001
Status code indicating a media domain whitelist check has failed.

@sl-slua/global/STRING_TRIM.documentation:
Value: 0x03
Trims whitespace off both the beginning and the end of the string.

@sl-slua/global/STRING_TRIM_HEAD.documentation:
Value: 0x01
Trims whitespace/spaces off the beginning of the string.

@sl-slua/global/STRING_TRIM_TAIL.documentation:
Value: 0x02
Trims whitespace/spaces off the end of the string.

@sl-slua/global/TARGETED_EMAIL_OBJECT_OW.documentation:
Value: 0x02
Flag used with llTargetedEmail to send the email message to the owner of the calling object.

@sl-slua/global/TARGETED_EMAIL_ROOT_CREA.documentation:
Value: 0x01
Flag used with llTargetedEmail to send the email message to the creator of the root object.

@sl-slua/global/TERRAIN_DETAIL_1.documentation:
Value: 0
Terrain parameter used with llSetGroundTexture to set the texture or material (via UUID, inventory name, or default NULL_KEY/empty string) for terrain detail layer 1.

@sl-slua/global/TERRAIN_DETAIL_2.documentation:
Value: 1
Terrain parameter used with llSetGroundTexture to set the texture or material (via UUID, inventory name, or default NULL_KEY/empty string) for terrain detail layer 2.

@sl-slua/global/TERRAIN_DETAIL_3.documentation:
Value: 2
Terrain parameter used with llSetGroundTexture to set the texture or material (via UUID, inventory name, or default NULL_KEY/empty string) for terrain detail layer 3.

@sl-slua/global/TERRAIN_DETAIL_4.documentation:
Value: 3
Terrain parameter used with llSetGroundTexture to set the texture or material (via UUID, inventory name, or default NULL_KEY/empty string) for terrain detail layer 4.

@sl-slua/global/TERRAIN_HEIGHT_RANGE_NE.documentation:
Value: 7
Terrain parameter used with llSetGroundTexture to set the north-east height range for texture blending. Specifies low as the maximum height for texture 1, high as the minimum height for texture 4, and lets textures 2 and 3 mix in between.

@sl-slua/global/TERRAIN_HEIGHT_RANGE_NW.documentation:
Value: 6
Terrain parameter used with llSetGroundTexture to set the north-west height range for texture blending. Specifies low as the maximum height for texture 1, high as the minimum height for texture 4, and lets textures 2 and 3 mix in between.

@sl-slua/global/TERRAIN_HEIGHT_RANGE_SE.documentation:
Value: 5
Terrain parameter used with llSetGroundTexture to set the south-east height range for texture blending. Specifies low as the maximum height for texture 1, high as the minimum height for texture 4, and lets textures 2 and 3 mix in between.

@sl-slua/global/TERRAIN_HEIGHT_RANGE_SW.documentation:
Value: 4
Terrain parameter used with llSetGroundTexture to set the south-west height range for texture blending. Specifies low as the maximum height for texture 1, high as the minimum height for texture 4, and lets textures 2 and 3 mix in between.

@sl-slua/global/TERRAIN_PBR_OFFSET_1.documentation:
Value: 16
Terrain parameter used with llSetGroundTexture to set the UV offset vector (ignoring the Z component) for drawing terrain layer 1. Only works for PBR textures.

@sl-slua/global/TERRAIN_PBR_OFFSET_2.documentation:
Value: 17
Terrain parameter used with llSetGroundTexture to set the UV offset vector (ignoring the Z component) for drawing terrain layer 2. Only works for PBR textures.

@sl-slua/global/TERRAIN_PBR_OFFSET_3.documentation:
Value: 18
Terrain parameter used with llSetGroundTexture to set the UV offset vector (ignoring the Z component) for drawing terrain layer 3. Only works for PBR textures.

@sl-slua/global/TERRAIN_PBR_OFFSET_4.documentation:
Value: 19
Terrain parameter used with llSetGroundTexture to set the UV offset vector (ignoring the Z component) for drawing terrain layer 4. Only works for PBR textures.

@sl-slua/global/TERRAIN_PBR_ROTATION_1.documentation:
Value: 12
Terrain parameter used with llSetGroundTexture to set the rotation of the PBR texture for layer 1, in radians. Only works for PBR textures.

@sl-slua/global/TERRAIN_PBR_ROTATION_2.documentation:
Value: 13
Terrain parameter used with llSetGroundTexture to set the rotation of the PBR texture for layer 2, in radians. Only works for PBR textures.

@sl-slua/global/TERRAIN_PBR_ROTATION_3.documentation:
Value: 14
Terrain parameter used with llSetGroundTexture to set the rotation of the PBR texture for layer 3, in radians. Only works for PBR textures.

@sl-slua/global/TERRAIN_PBR_ROTATION_4.documentation:
Value: 15
Terrain parameter used with llSetGroundTexture to set the rotation of the PBR texture for layer 4, in radians. Only works for PBR textures.

@sl-slua/global/TERRAIN_PBR_SCALE_1.documentation:
Value: 8
Terrain parameter used with llSetGroundTexture to set the UV scale vector (repeats per meter, ignoring the Z component) for terrain layer 1. Only works for PBR textures.

@sl-slua/global/TERRAIN_PBR_SCALE_2.documentation:
Value: 9
Terrain parameter used with llSetGroundTexture to set the UV scale vector (repeats per meter, ignoring the Z component) for terrain layer 2. Only works for PBR textures.

@sl-slua/global/TERRAIN_PBR_SCALE_3.documentation:
Value: 10
Terrain parameter used with llSetGroundTexture to set the UV scale vector (repeats per meter, ignoring the Z component) for terrain layer 3. Only works for PBR textures.

@sl-slua/global/TERRAIN_PBR_SCALE_4.documentation:
Value: 11
Terrain parameter used with llSetGroundTexture to set the UV scale vector (repeats per meter, ignoring the Z component) for terrain layer 4. Only works for PBR textures.

@sl-slua/global/TEXTURE_BLANK.documentation:
Value: uuid("5748decc-f629-461c-9a36-a35a221fe21f")
The asset UUID representing the default 'Blank' texture.

@sl-slua/global/TEXTURE_MEDIA.documentation:
Value: uuid("8b5fec65-8d8d-9dc5-cda8-8fdf2716e361")
The asset UUID representing the default 'Default Media' texture.

@sl-slua/global/TEXTURE_PLYWOOD.documentation:
Value: uuid("89556747-24cb-43ed-920b-47caed15465f")
The asset UUID representing the default 'Plywood' texture.

@sl-slua/global/TEXTURE_TRANSPARENT.documentation:
Value: uuid("8dcd4a48-2d37-4909-9f78-f7a9eb4ef903")
The asset UUID representing the '*Default Transparent Texture' in the library (included with viewers).

@sl-slua/global/TOUCH_INVALID_FACE.documentation:
Value: -1
Value returned by llDetectedTouchFace when the touch position is not valid.

@sl-slua/global/TOUCH_INVALID_TEXCOORD.documentation:
Value: vector(-1.0, -1.0, 0.0)
Value returned by llDetectedTouchUV and llDetectedTouchST when the touch position is not valid.

@sl-slua/global/TOUCH_INVALID_VECTOR.documentation:
Value: vector(0.0, 0.0, 0.0)
Value returned by llDetectedTouchPos, llDetectedTouchNormal, and llDetectedTouchBinormal when the touch position is not valid.

@sl-slua/global/TP_ROUTING_BLOCKED.documentation:
Value: 0
Teleport routing setting indicating that direct teleporting is blocked on the parcel.

@sl-slua/global/TP_ROUTING_FREE.documentation:
Value: 2
Teleport routing setting indicating that teleports are unrestricted on the parcel.

@sl-slua/global/TP_ROUTING_LANDINGP.documentation:
Value: 1
Teleport routing setting indicating that teleports are routed to the parcel's landing point (if one has been set).

@sl-slua/global/TRANSFER_BAD_OPTS.documentation:
Value: -1
Inventory transfer error code indicating that an invalid option was passed in the options list.

@sl-slua/global/TRANSFER_BAD_ROOT.documentation:
Value: -5
Inventory transfer error code indicating that the root path specified in TRANSFER_DEST was invalid or resolved to nothing.

@sl-slua/global/TRANSFER_DEST.documentation:
Value: 0
Inventory option specifying the destination root folder to transfer inventory into.

@sl-slua/global/TRANSFER_FLAGS.documentation:
Value: 1
Inventory parameter specifying flags to control the behavior of inventory transfers.

@sl-slua/global/TRANSFER_FLAG_COPY.documentation:
Value: 0x0004
Inventory transfer flag that copies the transferred object and places it in the recipient's inventory (implies TRANSFER_FLAG_TAKE).

@sl-slua/global/TRANSFER_FLAG_RESERVED.documentation:
Value: 0x0001
Inventory transfer flag reserved for future expansion; do not use.

@sl-slua/global/TRANSFER_FLAG_TAKE.documentation:
Value: 0x0002
Inventory transfer flag that automatically removes the object from the world and places it in the recipient's inventory once accepted.

@sl-slua/global/TRANSFER_NO_ATTACHMENT.documentation:
Value: -7
Inventory transfer error code indicating ownership of an attached object cannot be transferred.

@sl-slua/global/TRANSFER_NO_ITEMS.documentation:
Value: -4
Inventory transfer error code indicating that the list was empty or contained only non-transferable items.

@sl-slua/global/TRANSFER_NO_PERMS.documentation:
Value: -6
Inventory transfer error code indicating the object lacks transfer permissions.

@sl-slua/global/TRANSFER_NO_TARGET.documentation:
Value: -2
Inventory transfer error code indicating the receiving agent could not be found in the current region.

@sl-slua/global/TRANSFER_OK.documentation:
Value: 0
Status code indicating that the inventory transfer was successful or the transfer offer was successfully made.

@sl-slua/global/TRANSFER_THROTTLE.documentation:
Value: -3
Inventory transfer error code indicating that the transfer rate has exceeded the inventory transfer throttle.

@sl-slua/global/TRAVERSAL_TYPE.documentation:
Value: 7
Pathfinding parameter specifying the character's movement traversal type. Expects TRAVERSAL_TYPE_SLOW (default), _FAST, or _NONE.

@sl-slua/global/TWO_PI.documentation:
Value: 6.28318530
The mathematical constant representing the number of radians in a full circle (6.28318530, twice the value of PI).

@sl-slua/global/TYPE_FLOAT.documentation:
Value: 2
Variable type constant indicating the list entry or value is a float.

@sl-slua/global/TYPE_INTEGER.documentation:
Value: 1
Variable type constant indicating the list entry or value is an integer.

@sl-slua/global/TYPE_INVALID.documentation:
Value: 0
Variable type constant indicating the list entry or value is invalid.

@sl-slua/global/TYPE_KEY.documentation:
Value: 4
Variable type constant indicating the list entry or value is a key.

@sl-slua/global/TYPE_ROTATION.documentation:
Value: 6
Variable type constant indicating the list entry or value is a rotation.

@sl-slua/global/TYPE_STRING.documentation:
Value: 3
Variable type constant indicating the list entry or value is a string.

@sl-slua/global/TYPE_VECTOR.documentation:
Value: 5
Variable type constant indicating the list entry or value is a vector.

@sl-slua/global/VEHICLE_ANGULAR_DEFLECTI.documentation:
Value: 32
Vehicle float parameter (range 0.0 to 1.0) acting as a scalar to modulate the strength of angular deflection, reorienting the vehicle's preferred axis toward its true velocity.

Value: 33
Vehicle float parameter specifying the exponential timescale for the vehicle to achieve full angular deflection, reorienting its preferred axis of motion to match its true velocity.

@sl-slua/global/VEHICLE_ANGULAR_FRICTION.documentation:
Value: 17
Vehicle vector parameter specifying the timescales (range [0.07, infinity) seconds per axis) for the exponential decay of angular velocity about the vehicle's preferred axes (at, left, up).

@sl-slua/global/VEHICLE_ANGULAR_MOTOR_DE.documentation:
Value: 35
Vehicle float parameter specifying the exponential timescale (in seconds) for the angular motor's magnitude and effectiveness to decay toward zero.

@sl-slua/global/VEHICLE_ANGULAR_MOTOR_DI.documentation:
Value: 19
Vehicle vector parameter specifying the direction and magnitude of the angular velocity (in radians per second) that the vehicle's angular motor attempts to achieve.

@sl-slua/global/VEHICLE_ANGULAR_MOTOR_TI.documentation:
Value: 34
Vehicle float parameter specifying the exponential timescale for the vehicle's angular motor to achieve full power and velocity.

@sl-slua/global/VEHICLE_BANKING_EFFICIEN.documentation:
Value: 38
Vehicle float parameter (range -1.0 to 1.0) controlling banking efficiency, where negative values lean out of turns and positive values lean into turns. This parameter makes banking affect steering; use angular motors to bank. 0.0 means no banking.

@sl-slua/global/VEHICLE_BANKING_MIX.documentation:
Value: 39
Vehicle float parameter (range 0.0 (static) to 1.0) (dynamic) controlling the mix between static banking (scales only with roll angle) and dynamic banking (additionally scales with linear speed).

@sl-slua/global/VEHICLE_BANKING_TIMESCAL.documentation:
Value: 40
Vehicle float parameter specifying the exponential timescale for the banking behavior to take full effect. This is another way to scale the strength of the banking effect, however it affects the term that is proportional to the difference between what the banking behavior is trying to do, and what the vehicle is actually doing.

@sl-slua/global/VEHICLE_BUOYANCY.documentation:
Value: 27
Vehicle float parameter (range -1.0 to 1.0) defining buoyancy, where -1.0 represents double gravity and 1.0 represents full anti-gravity.

@sl-slua/global/VEHICLE_FLAG_BLOCK_INTER.documentation:
Value: 0x400
Vehicle flag that prevents attachments worn by passengers from pushing the vehicle via llPushObject or other scripting functions.

@sl-slua/global/VEHICLE_FLAG_CAMERA_DECO.documentation:
Value: 0x200
Vehicle flag used with mouselook steering/banking. When set, the passenger's mouselook camera rotates independently of the vehicle's orientation.

@sl-slua/global/VEHICLE_FLAG_HOVER_GLOBA.documentation:
Value: 0x10
Vehicle flag that forces the hover behavior to maintain a global height rather than hovering relative to the ground or water.

@sl-slua/global/VEHICLE_FLAG_HOVER_TERRA.documentation:
Value: 0x8
Vehicle flag that forces the hover behavior to ignore water height and hover exclusively relative to the terrain/land.

@sl-slua/global/VEHICLE_FLAG_HOVER_UP_ON.documentation:
Value: 0x20
Vehicle flag preventing hover from pushing downward, allowing hovering vehicles to jump or fly above their designated hover height.

@sl-slua/global/VEHICLE_FLAG_HOVER_WATER.documentation:
Value: 0x4
Vehicle flag that forces the hover behavior to ignore terrain height and hover exclusively relative to water.
